<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Outline_of_political_science#Politics_by_region" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Outline of political science">Other countries</a></li></ul>
</div></td></tr><tr><td class="sidebar-navbar"><span class="mw-empty-elt"><link about="#mwt39" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Hlist/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333133064" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/><style about="#mwt40" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Navbar/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1239400231" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles">.mw-parser-output .navbar{display:inline;font-size:88%;font-weight:normal}.mw-parser-output .navbar-collapse{float:left;text-align:left}.mw-parser-output .navbar-boxtext{word-spacing:0}.mw-parser-output .navbar ul{display:inline-block;white-space:nowrap;line-height:inherit}.mw-parser-output .navbar-brackets::before{margin-right:-0.125em;content:"[ "}.mw-parser-output .navbar-brackets::after{margin-left:-0.125em;content:" ]"}.mw-parser-output .navbar li{word-spacing:-0.125em}.mw-parser-output .navbar a>span,.mw-parser-output .navbar a>abbr{text-decoration:inherit}.mw-parser-output .navbar-mini abbr{font-variant:small-caps;border-bottom:none;text-decoration:none;cursor:inherit}.mw-parser-output .navbar-ct-full{font-size:114%;margin:0 7em}.mw-parser-output .navbar-ct-mini{font-size:114%;margin:0 4em}html.skin-theme-clientpref-night .mw-parser-output .navbar li a abbr{color:var(--color-base)!important}@media(prefers-color-scheme:dark){html.skin-theme-clientpref-os .mw-parser-output .navbar li a abbr{color:var(--color-base)!important}}@media print{.mw-parser-output .navbar{display:none!important}}</style></span><div class="navbar plainlinks hlist navbar-mini"><ul><li class="nv-view"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template:Politics_of_the_Philippines" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Template:Politics of the Philippines"><abbr title="View this template">v</abbr></a></li><li class="nv-talk"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Politics_of_the_Philippines" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Template talk:Politics of the Philippines"><abbr title="Discuss this template">t</abbr></a></li><li class="nv-edit"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Politics_of_the_Philippines" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Special:EditPage/Template:Politics of the Philippines"><abbr title="Edit this template">e</abbr></a></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
<p id="mwEQ">Presidential elections in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippines" id="mwEg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippines">Philippines</a> were held on May 9, 2016, as part of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_general_election" id="mwEw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine general election">2016 general election</a>. This was the 16th direct presidential election in the country since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1935_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwFA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1935 Philippine presidential election">1935</a> and the fifth <a class="extiw" href="https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/sextennial" id="mwFQ" rel="mw:WikiLink/Interwiki" title="wikt:sextennial">sextennial</a> presidential election since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwFg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">1992</a>.</p>
<p id="mwFw">Incumbent president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwGA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a> was ineligible for re-election, pursuant to the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwGQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">1987 Philippine Constitution</a>. Incumbent vice president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwGg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a> was eligible for re-election but chose to run for the presidency instead. Therefore, this election determined the 16th <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwGw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="President of the Philippines">president</a> and the 14th <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vice_President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwHA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vice President of the Philippines">vice president</a>. The position of president and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vice_President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwHQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vice President of the Philippines">vice president</a> are elected separately, thus the two winning candidates could come from different political parties.</p>
<p id="mwHg"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwHw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> was elected President of the Philippines with 38.5% of the vote.<sup about="#mwt43" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-2"}}' id="cite_ref-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-2" id="mwIA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwIQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwIg">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwIw">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congress_of_the_Philippines" id="mwJA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congress of the Philippines">Congress</a> met in late May to canvass the results and issued an official result with Duterte and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwJQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> emerging as the winners of the presidential and vice presidential races, respectively. They were proclaimed on May 30 in the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House_of_Representatives_(Philippines)" id="mwJg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="House of Representatives (Philippines)">House of Representatives</a>.</p>
<p id="mwJw">On the other hand, Robredo narrowly won the vice presidential election over <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwKA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>, son of former president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwKQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">Ferdinand Marcos</a>. Bongbong Marcos filed an electoral protest through the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Electoral_Tribunal" id="mwKg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential Electoral Tribunal">Presidential Electoral Tribunal</a>, but was later dismissed in 2021.</p>
<meta data-mw='{"autoGenerated":true}' id="mwKw" property="mw:PageProp/toc"/></section><section aria-labelledby="Electoral_system" data-mw-section-id="1" id="mwLA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Electoral_system">Electoral system</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=1" title="Edit section: Electoral system"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt44" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwLQ" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><style about="#mwt45" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1353705441" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles">.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+span.mw-empty-elt+.hatnote,.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}</style></span><div about="#mwt44" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwLg" role="note">Main article: <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine presidential election">Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwLw">According to the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwMA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">Constitution of the Philippines</a>, the election is held every six years after 1992, on the second Monday of May. The incumbent president is term limited. The incumbent vice president may run for two consecutive terms. The <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plurality_voting_system" id="mwMQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Plurality voting system">plurality voting system</a> is used to determine the winner: the candidate with the highest number of votes, whether or not one has a majority, wins the presidency. The vice presidential election is a separate election, is held on the same rules, and voters may split their ticket. Both winners will serve six-year terms commencing on the noon of June 30, 2016, and ending on the same day six years later.<sup about="#mwt48" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-3"}}' id="cite_ref-3" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-3" id="mwMg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwMw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwNA">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwNQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Background" data-mw-section-id="2" id="mwNg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Background">Background</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=2" title="Edit section: Background"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt49" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"see also","href":"./Template:See_also"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"2010 Philippine presidential election"},"2":{"wt":"2013 Philippine Senate election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwNw" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt50" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt49" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwOA" role="note">See also: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2010 Philippine presidential election">2010 Philippine presidential election</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Philippine_Senate_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Philippine Senate election">2013 Philippine Senate election</a></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwOQ" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href='https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Benigno_"Noynoy"_S._Aquino_III_(cropped).jpg' id="mwOg"><img class="mw-file-element mw-file-upright" data-file-height="858" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="648" decoding="async" height="252" id="mwOw" resource='https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Benigno_"Noynoy"_S._Aquino_III_(cropped).jpg' src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Benigno_%22Noynoy%22_S._Aquino_III_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Benigno_%22Noynoy%22_S._Aquino_III_%28cropped%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Benigno_%22Noynoy%22_S._Aquino_III_%28cropped%29.jpg/500px-Benigno_%22Noynoy%22_S._Aquino_III_%28cropped%29.jpg 2x" style="--mw-file-upright: 0.75" width="190"/></a><figcaption id="mwPA"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwPQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a>, the outgoing president, whose term expired on June 30, 2016</figcaption></figure>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwPg" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png" id="mwPw"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwQA" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png/250px-2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png/500px-2010PhilippineVPElectionResults.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwQQ">Map of the results of the 2010 vice presidential election.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwQg">Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwQw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a> of the Liberal Party, who ran on an anti-corruption platform, won the 2010 election with 42.08% of the votes defeating <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Estrada" id="mwRA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Joseph Estrada">Joseph Estrada</a>, a former president who was deposed in 2001 after scandals of massive corruption, and several others. Meanwhile, Estrada's running mate, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwRQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a> Mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwRg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a> of the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Demokratiko_Pilipino-Lakas_ng_Bayan" id="mwRw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Demokratiko Pilipino-Lakas ng Bayan">Partido Demokratiko Pilipino-Lakas ng Bayan</a> (PDP–Laban), defeated Aquino's running mate, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwSA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a> of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwSQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a> and several others, in the vice presidential election.<sup about="#mwt53" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"June 9"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-June_9-4"}}' id="cite_ref-June_9_4-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-June_9-4" id="mwSg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwSw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwTA">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwTQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Roxas eventually accused Binay of electoral fraud in the Presidential Electoral Tribunal, citing that some of his votes were recorded as <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Null_vote" id="mwTg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Null vote">null votes</a>.<sup about="#mwt56" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Bordadora2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Bordadora2-5"}}' id="cite_ref-Bordadora2_5-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Bordadora2-5" id="mwTw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwUA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUQ">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwUw">Both Binay and Roxas were subsequently appointed by Aquino to his cabinet, with Binay heading the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Housing_and_Urban_Development_Coordinating_Council" id="mwVA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Housing and Urban Development Coordinating Council">Housing and Urban Development Coordinating Council</a>,<sup about="#mwt59" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-6"}}' id="cite_ref-6" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-6" id="mwVQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwVg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwVw">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwWA">]</span></span></a></sup> and Roxas first being given the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Transportation_(Philippines)" id="mwWQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Department of Transportation (Philippines)">Transportation and Communications</a> portfolio, then finally named as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_the_Interior_and_Local_Government" id="mwWg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Secretary of the Interior and Local Government">Secretary of the Interior and Local Government</a>, after the ban of appointing losing candidates expired a year after Aquino took office on June 30, 2010.<sup about="#mwt62" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-7"}}' id="cite_ref-7" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-7" id="mwWw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwXA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXQ">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwXg">]</span></span></a></sup> As of 2014, the tribunal had still not yet acted upon the preliminary motions of both parties and on Binay's counter-protest; the suit is expected to still not have been resolved by the time President Aquino's term expires.<sup about="#mwt65" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-8"}}' id="cite_ref-8" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-8" id="mwXw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwYA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwYQ">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwYg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwYw">For the midterm <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwZA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Philippine Senate election">2013 Senate election</a>, Aquino and Roxas formed the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Team_PNoy" id="mwZQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Team PNoy">Team PNoy</a> coalition;<sup about="#mwt69" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-9"}}' id="cite_ref-9" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-9" id="mwZg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwZw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwaA">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwaQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Estrada's PMP and Binay's PDP–Laban forged an <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electoral_alliance" id="mwag" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Electoral alliance">electoral alliance</a>, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nationalist_Alliance" id="mwaw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="United Nationalist Alliance">United Nationalist Alliance</a> (UNA).<sup about="#mwt72" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-10"}}' id="cite_ref-10" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-10" id="mwbA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwbQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwbg">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwbw">]</span></span></a></sup> Team PNoy won nine Senate seats against UNA's three.<sup about="#mwt75" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-11"}}' id="cite_ref-11" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-11" id="mwcA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwcQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwcg">[</span>11<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwcw">]</span></span></a></sup> Former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Movie_and_Television_Review_and_Classification_Board" id="mwdA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Movie and Television Review and Classification Board">Movie and Television Review and Classification Board</a> chairwoman <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwdQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>, daughter of defeated 2004 presidential candidate <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fernando_Poe_Jr." id="mwdg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Fernando Poe Jr.">Fernando Poe Jr.</a> topped the senatorial election.<sup about="#mwt78" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-12"}}' id="cite_ref-12" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-12" id="mwdw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mweA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mweQ">[</span>12<span class="cite-bracket" id="mweg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwew">In March 2014, PDP–Laban withdrew from UNA, a week after Binay resigned as party chairman, due "to differences with its leaders". Party president <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aquilino_Pimentel_III" id="mwfA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aquilino Pimentel III">Aquilino Pimentel III</a> had a public quarrel with Binay over <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Migz_Zubiri" id="mwfQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Migz Zubiri">Migz Zubiri</a>'s inclusion in UNA's 2013 senatorial slate, whom Pimentel had accused of cheating in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2007_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwfg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2007 Philippine Senate election">2007 Senate election</a>.<sup about="#mwt81" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-13"}}' id="cite_ref-13" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-13" id="mwfw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwgA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwgQ">[</span>13<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwgg">]</span></span></a></sup> In Pulse Asia survey released in April 2014, the first opinion poll for the presidential election, it showed Binay leading aspiring candidates, with 40% of those surveyed with Grace Poe a far second at 15%.<sup about="#mwt84" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Pulse Asia March 2014"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Pulse_Asia_March_2014-14"}}' id="cite_ref-Pulse_Asia_March_2014_14-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Pulse_Asia_March_2014-14" id="mwgw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwhA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhQ">[</span>14<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwhw">Several other stalwarts of UNA, such as senators <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Juan_Ponce_Enrile" id="mwiA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Juan Ponce Enrile">Juan Ponce Enrile</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jinggoy_Estrada" id="mwiQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jinggoy Estrada">Jinggoy Estrada</a>, who had manifested his intention to run as Binay's running mate, and others such as Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bong_Revilla" id="mwig" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bong Revilla">Bong Revilla</a> of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakas–CMD" id="mwiw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lakas–CMD">Lakas–CMD</a>, who was planning to run for president, were detained due to their involvement in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pork_barrel_scam" id="mwjA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pork barrel scam">pork barrel scam</a>.<sup about="#mwt87" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-15"}}' id="cite_ref-15" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-15" id="mwjQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwjg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwjw">[</span>15<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwkA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt90" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-16"}}' id="cite_ref-16" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-16" id="mwkQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwkg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwkw">[</span>16<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwlA">]</span></span></a></sup> No personalities linked with the Liberal Party involved in the scam were investigated. These actions by the government, which it said was part of its anti-corruption drive, were cited by UNA as "political persecution".<sup about="#mwt93" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-17"}}' id="cite_ref-17" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-17" id="mwlQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwlg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwlw">[</span>17<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwmA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwmQ">In July 2014, Renato Bondal, a defeated mayoral candidate in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Makati_local_elections" id="mwmg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Makati local elections">2013 Makati mayoral election</a>, filed plunder cases against Makati mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay_Jr." id="mwmw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay Jr.">Jejomar Binay, Jr.</a> and his father, the vice president, to the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ombudsman_of_the_Philippines" id="mwnA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ombudsman of the Philippines">Ombudsman</a>. By the next month, a subcommittee of the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Senate_Blue_Ribbon_Committee" id="mwnQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Senate Blue Ribbon Committee">Blue Ribbon Committee</a> composed solely of Pimentel, along with <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwng" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a> members <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwnw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> and <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes_IV" id="mwoA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes IV">Antonio Trillanes IV</a>, began Senate hearings against Binay on his alleged corruption while serving as mayor of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwoQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a>, beginning with the alleged overpriced annex building of the Makati City Hall.<sup about="#mwt96" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-18"}}' id="cite_ref-18" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-18" id="mwog"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwow"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwpA">[</span>18<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwpQ">]</span></span></a></sup> It was followed by hearings on alleged corruption on deals supplying Makati senior citizens with birthday cakes,<sup about="#mwt99" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-19"}}' id="cite_ref-19" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-19" id="mwpg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwpw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwqA">[</span>19<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwqQ">]</span></span></a></sup> an agricultural estate in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosario,_Batangas" id="mwqg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rosario, Batangas">Rosario, Batangas</a> that Binay allegedly owns,<sup about="#mwt102" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-20"}}' id="cite_ref-20" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-20" id="mwqw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwrA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwrQ">[</span>20<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwrg">]</span></span></a></sup> the allegedly overpriced <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati_Science_High_School" id="mwrw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati Science High School">Makati Science High School</a>,<sup about="#mwt105" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-21"}}' id="cite_ref-21" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-21" id="mwsA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwsQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwsg">[</span>21<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwsw">]</span></span></a></sup> and the relocation of Makati residents to <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calauan,_Laguna" id="mwtA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Calauan, Laguna">Calauan, Laguna</a> in a community without basic necessities.<sup about="#mwt108" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-22"}}' id="cite_ref-22" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-22" id="mwtQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwtg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwtw">[</span>22<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwuA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwuQ" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_(2014)_a0001.JPG" id="mwug"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2736" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3648" decoding="async" height="188" id="mwuw" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_(2014)_a0001.JPG" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/82/PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_%282014%29_a0001.JPG/250px-PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_%282014%29_a0001.JPG" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/82/PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_%282014%29_a0001.JPG/500px-PH-MM-makati-j.p._rizal_street-city_hall_-_pasig_river_view_%282014%29_a0001.JPG 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwvA">Makati City Hall (taller building to the right) as viewed from the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasig_River" id="mwvQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasig River">Pasig River</a>; the allegedly overpriced annex is the building to the left.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwvg">Binay had consistently denied any wrongdoing,<sup about="#mwt111" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-23"}}' id="cite_ref-23" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-23" id="mwvw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwwA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwwQ">[</span>23<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwwg">]</span></span></a></sup> and from owning the Rosario estate,<sup about="#mwt114" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-24"}}' id="cite_ref-24" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-24" id="mwww"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwxA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwxQ">[</span>24<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwxg">]</span></span></a></sup> but had never appeared at the Senate.<sup about="#mwt117" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-25"}}' id="cite_ref-25" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-25" id="mwxw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwyA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwyQ">[</span>25<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwyg">]</span></span></a></sup> UNA Secretary General JV Bautista branded the investigations as part of the "Operation Plan Stop Nognog", insinuating on Binay's dark skin, with Roxas, Cayetano and Trillanes allegedly behind it to prevent Binay from becoming president. He accused billionaire businessman Salvador Zamora as its financier.<sup about="#mwt120" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-26"}}' id="cite_ref-26" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-26" id="mwyw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwzA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwzQ">[</span>26<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwzg">]</span></span></a></sup> In May 2015, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Court_of_Appeals_of_the_Philippines" id="mwzw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Court of Appeals of the Philippines">Court of Appeals</a> ordered the 242 bank accounts belonging to Binay to be frozen for six months, when it granted the petition of the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-Money_Laundering_Council_(Philippines)" id="mw0A" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Anti-Money Laundering Council (Philippines)">Anti-Money Laundering Council</a> and of the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ombudsman_of_the_Philippines" id="mw0Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ombudsman of the Philippines">Ombudsman</a>. Binay's camp had alleged certain people from the Liberal Party to be behind the freeze order,<sup about="#mwt123" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-27"}}' id="cite_ref-27" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-27" id="mw0g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw0w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1A">[</span>27<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1Q">]</span></span></a></sup> a charge President Aquino, in a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bombo_Radyo_Philippines" id="mw1g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bombo Radyo Philippines">Bombo Radyo</a> interview, himself denied.<sup about="#mwt126" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-28"}}' id="cite_ref-28" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-28" id="mw1w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw2A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw2Q">[</span>28<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw2g">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw2w">By late May 2015, the subcommittee report recommending the filing of a plunder (corruption worth more than 50 million pesos) complaint against Binay was signed by all three subcommittee members and Grace Poe.<sup about="#mwt129" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-29"}}' id="cite_ref-29" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-29" id="mw3A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw3Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw3g">[</span>29<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw3w">]</span></span></a></sup> By early June, ten senators had already signed the subcommittee report, making it official and available to be debated upon in the Senate floor.<sup about="#mwt132" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-30"}}' id="cite_ref-30" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-30" id="mw4A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw4Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw4g">[</span>30<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw4w">]</span></span></a></sup> A Pulse Asia presidential survey taken at the time the hearings were done showed Grace Poe overtaking Binay, with her getting 30% of the respondents, over Binay's 22%.<sup about="#mwt135" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Pulse June2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Pulse_June2-31"}}' id="cite_ref-Pulse_June2_31-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Pulse_June2-31" id="mw5A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw5Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw5g">[</span>31<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw5w">]</span></span></a></sup> A month later, Binay sued Cayetano, Trillanes and several others for 200 million pesos in damages at the Makati Regional Trial Court for "well-organized and orchestrated effort" to damage his reputation and worsen his chances of becoming president.<sup about="#mwt138" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-32"}}' id="cite_ref-32" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-32" id="mw6A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw6Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw6g">[</span>32<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw6w">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw7A">Meanwhile, Aquino held several meetings with Roxas, Poe and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Escudero" id="mw7Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Escudero">Francis Escudero</a> on who should be the standard bearer of the Liberal Party. While none of them had announced their preferences at that time, Aquino was expected to announce his preferred candidate after his final State of the Nation Address late in July. Congressional heads Franklin Drilon and Feliciano Belmonte, Jr. denied that Liberal Party members had been dissatisfied with Aquino's indecision, saying that the party was still united.<sup about="#mwt141" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-33"}}' id="cite_ref-33" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-33" id="mw7g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw7w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw8A">[</span>33<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw8Q">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw8g">On early July, Binay launched his party, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nationalist_Alliance" id="mw8w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="United Nationalist Alliance">United Nationalist Alliance</a>.<sup about="#mwt144" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Flores-2015"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Flores-2015-34"}}' id="cite_ref-Flores-2015_34-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Flores-2015-34" id="mw9A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw9Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw9g">[</span>34<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw9w">]</span></span></a></sup> Later that month, Aquino endorsed Roxas for president, which the latter accepted.<sup about="#mwt147" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Alvarez-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Alvarez-2015a-35"}}' id="cite_ref-Alvarez-2015a_35-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Alvarez-2015a-35" id="mw-A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw-Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw-g">[</span>35<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw-w">]</span></span></a></sup> In August, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mw_A" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a>, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mw_Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> mayor who had been a subject of a strong online following urging him to run, announced his intention to retire from politics after his mayoral term ends in 2016.<sup about="#mwt150" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-36"}}' id="cite_ref-36" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-36" id="mw_g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw_w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQA">[</span>36<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQE">]</span></span></a></sup> Poe announced her intention to seek the presidency by mid-September,<sup about="#mwt153" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-37"}}' id="cite_ref-37" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-37" id="mwAQI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQQ">[</span>37<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQU">]</span></span></a></sup> followed by Escudero's announcing that he'll be her running mate a day later.<sup about="#mwt156" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Ager-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Ager-2015a-38"}}' id="cite_ref-Ager-2015a_38-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Ager-2015a-38" id="mwAQY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQg">[</span>38<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQk">]</span></span></a></sup> Several days later, Cayetano announced his vice presidential candidacy, preferring to be Duterte's running mate.</p>
<p id="mwAQo">On October 3. Trillanes formally announced his vice presidential campaign as an independent, supporting Poe's presidential campaign.<sup about="#mwt159" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-39"}}' id="cite_ref-39" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-39" id="mwAQs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQ0">[</span>39<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQ4">]</span></span></a></sup> Days later, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwAQ8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a>, the representative from Camarines Sur and widow of former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_the_Interior_and_Local_Government" id="mwARA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Secretary of the Interior and Local Government">Secretary of the Interior and Local Government</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jesse_Robredo" id="mwARE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jesse Robredo">Jesse Robredo</a> who died in a <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_Philippine_Piper_Seneca_crash" id="mwARI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2012 Philippine Piper Seneca crash">plane crash</a> in 2012, accepted the offer of the Liberal Party to be Roxas' running mate.<sup about="#mwt162" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Cruz-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Cruz-2015a-40"}}' id="cite_ref-Cruz-2015a_40-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Cruz-2015a-40" id="mwARM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwARQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARU">[</span>40<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARY">]</span></span></a></sup> Also on that day, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwARc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> announced his candidacy as vice president.<sup about="#mwt165" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"CNN-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-CNN-2015a-41"}}' id="cite_ref-CNN-2015a_41-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-CNN-2015a-41" id="mwARg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwARk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARo">[</span>41<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARs">]</span></span></a></sup> A week later, after being nominated by UNA, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gregorio_Honasan" id="mwARw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gregorio Honasan">Gregorio Honasan</a> announced that he would be Binay's running mate.<sup about="#mwt168" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Mallari-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Mallari-2015a-42"}}' id="cite_ref-Mallari-2015a_42-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Mallari-2015a-42" id="mwAR0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAR4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAR8">[</span>42<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASA">]</span></span></a></sup> A day later, on the launch of her new book, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago" id="mwASE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago">Miriam Defensor Santiago</a> announced her presidential candidacy;<sup about="#mwt171" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Bartolome-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Bartolome-2015a-43"}}' id="cite_ref-Bartolome-2015a_43-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Bartolome-2015a-43" id="mwASI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwASM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASQ">[</span>43<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASU">]</span></span></a></sup> a couple of days later, she announced that Marcos would be her running mate.<sup about="#mwt174" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Hegina-2015a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Hegina-2015a-44"}}' id="cite_ref-Hegina-2015a_44-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Hegina-2015a-44" id="mwASY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwASc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASg">[</span>44<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwASo">At the final day of the filing of candidacies, Duterte did not show up; instead, the PDP–Laban nominated Martin Diño, chairman of the Volunteers Against Crime and Corruption.<sup about="#mwt177" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"GMA News-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-GMA_News-2015b-45"}}' id="cite_ref-GMA_News-2015b_45-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-GMA_News-2015b-45" id="mwASs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwASw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAS0">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAS4">]</span></span></a></sup> At the end of the day, more than a hundred people registered as presidential candidates, after a 5-day long filing period. These include current OFW Family Club representative <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roy_Señeres" id="mwAS8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Roy Señeres">Roy Señeres</a> running under the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_ng_Manggagawa_at_Magsasaka" id="mwATA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido ng Manggagawa at Magsasaka">Partido ng Manggagawa at Magsasaka</a>, former representative from Iloilo Augusto Syjuco, who is running as an independent, and Engineer Juanita Mendoza Trocenio under the Partido Bagong Maharlika (PBM). For the vice presidency, nineteen people manifested their intention to run, including former assemblyman from Ifugao <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zosimo_Jesus_Paredes_II" id="mwATE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zosimo Jesus Paredes II">Zosimo Jesus Paredes II</a>, who is also running under the Partido Bagong Maharlika.</p>
<p id="mwATI">AC Nielsen Philippines showed on its monitoring report between Jan 1 and Nov 30 that presidential candidate Binay spent 595 million pesos for campaign advertisements on television (before the campaign period). Vice presidential candidate Cayetano led the spenders in the race, spending 398 million pesos worth of TV advertisements.<sup about="#mwt180" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-46"}}' id="cite_ref-46" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-46" id="mwATM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATU">[</span>46<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATY">]</span></span></a></sup> Binay, denied the claim.<sup about="#mwt183" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-47"}}' id="cite_ref-47" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-47" id="mwATc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATk">[</span>47<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATo">]</span></span></a></sup> An article from The <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila_Times" id="mwATs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila Times">Manila Times</a> reported also that the administration's bet, Mar Roxas, led the list of the biggest ad spenders in the country with 774 million peso in television advertisement expenses from January to December, in a survey also conducted by AC Nielsen.<sup about="#mwt186" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-48"}}' id="cite_ref-48" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-48" id="mwATw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAT0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAT4">[</span>48<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAT8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Candidates" data-mw-section-id="3" id="mwAUA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Candidates">Candidates</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=3" title="Edit section: Candidates"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt187" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Candidates in the 2016 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAUE" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt188" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt187" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwAUI" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Candidates_in_the_2016_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Candidates in the 2016 Philippine presidential election">Candidates in the 2016 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwAUM">Under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAUQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">Constitution of the Philippines</a>, the President and Vice President are elected separately. An initial list of eight presidential candidates were uploaded on the certified candidates list in the commission's database on January 21, 2016.<sup about="#mwt191" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Candidates"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Candidates-49"}}' id="cite_ref-Candidates_49-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Candidates-49" id="mwAUU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAUY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUc">[</span>49<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUg">]</span></span></a></sup> But only trimmed down to five, due to the declaration by the commission en banc of Dante Valencia as a nuisance candidate and the withdrawal of certificates of candidacies of Romel Mendoza and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roy_Señeres" id="mwAUk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Roy Señeres">Roy Señeres</a>.<sup about="#mwt194" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-50"}}' id="cite_ref-50" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-50" id="mwAUo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAUs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUw">[</span>50<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAU0">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt197" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-51"}}' id="cite_ref-51" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-51" id="mwAU4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAU8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVA">[</span>51<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt200" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-52"}}' id="cite_ref-52" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-52" id="mwAVI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAVM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVQ">[</span>52<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAVY">This is the final and certified list of candidates for the elections to be included in the ballots:<sup about="#mwt203" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-53"}}' id="cite_ref-53" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-53" id="mwAVc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAVg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVk">[</span>53<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVo">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt206" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-54"}}' id="cite_ref-54" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-54" id="mwAVs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAVw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAV0">[</span>54<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAV4">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwAV8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a>,<sup about="#mwt209" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-55"}}' id="cite_ref-55" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-55" id="mwAWA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWI">[</span>55<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWM">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago" id="mwAWQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago">Miriam Defensor Santiago</a>,<sup about="#mwt212" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-56"}}' id="cite_ref-56" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-56" id="mwAWU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWc">[</span>56<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWg">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwAWk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a>,<sup about="#mwt215" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-57"}}' id="cite_ref-57" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-57" id="mwAWo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWw">[</span>57<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAW0">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt218" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-58"}}' id="cite_ref-58" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-58" id="mwAW4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAW8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXA">[</span>58<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXE">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwAXI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>,<sup about="#mwt221" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-59"}}' id="cite_ref-59" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-59" id="mwAXM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAXQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXU">[</span>59<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXY">]</span></span></a></sup>  and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwAXc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a>.<sup about="#mwt224" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-60"}}' id="cite_ref-60" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-60" id="mwAXg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAXk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXo">[</span>60<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXs">]</span></span></a></sup> Although Señeres withdrew his candidacy and later died, his name was still included on the printed ballots.</p>

<td id="mwAcA">Senator</td>

<span about="#mwt246" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"notelist","href":"./Template:Notelist"},"params":{},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAjo" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><style about="#mwt247" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Reflist/styles.css"}}' data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1327269900" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles">.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-2{column-width:30em}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-3{column-width:25em}body.skin-vector-2022 .mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-2{column-width:27em}body.skin-vector-2022 .mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-3{column-width:22.5em}.mw-parser-output .references[data-mw-group=upper-alpha]{list-style-type:upper-alpha}.mw-parser-output .references[data-mw-group=upper-roman]{list-style-type:upper-roman}.mw-parser-output .references[data-mw-group=lower-alpha]{list-style-type:lower-alpha}.mw-parser-output .references[data-mw-group=lower-greek]{list-style-type:lower-greek}.mw-parser-output .references[data-mw-group=lower-roman]{list-style-type:lower-roman}.mw-parser-output div.reflist-liststyle-upper-alpha .references{list-style-type:upper-alpha}.mw-parser-output div.reflist-liststyle-upper-roman .references{list-style-type:upper-roman}.mw-parser-output div.reflist-liststyle-lower-alpha .references{list-style-type:lower-alpha}.mw-parser-output div.reflist-liststyle-lower-greek .references{list-style-type:lower-greek}.mw-parser-output div.reflist-liststyle-lower-roman .references{list-style-type:lower-roman}</style></span><div about="#mwt246" id="mwAjs">
<div about="#mwt248" class="mw-references-wrap" data-mw='{"name":"references","attrs":{"group":"lower-alpha","responsive":"1"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' id="mwAjw" typeof="mw:Extension/references"><ol class="mw-references references" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mwAj0"><li about="#cite_note-fn1-61" data-mw-footnote-number="a" id="cite_note-fn1-61"><span class="mw-cite-backlink" id="mwAj4" rel="mw:referencedBy"><a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-fn1_61-0" id="mwAj8"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwAkA">1</span></a> <a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-fn1_61-1" id="mwAkE"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwAkI">2</span></a> <a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-fn1_61-2" id="mwAkM"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwAkQ">3</span></a></span> <span class="mw-reference-text reference-text" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mw-reference-text-cite_note-fn1-61">Cayetano, Marcos and Trillanes are all members of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwAkU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>, but failed to get that party's nomination; instead, all of them ran as independents.</span></li>
</ol></div></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Jejomar_Binay_(UNA)" data-mw-section-id="4" id="mwAkY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Jejomar_Binay_(UNA)"><span id="Jejomar_Binay_.28UNA.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Jejomar Binay (UNA)</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=4" title="Edit section: Jejomar Binay (UNA)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt249" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Jejomar Binay 2016 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAkc" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt250" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt249" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwAkg" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay_2016_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay 2016 presidential campaign">Jejomar Binay 2016 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwAkk">When questioned by the media at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coconut_Palace" id="mwAko" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Coconut Palace">Coconut Palace</a> in September 2011, Vice President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwAks" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP–Laban" id="mwAkw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PDP–Laban">PDP–Laban</a>) confirmed his plans of running as president.<sup about="#mwt253" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-62"}}' id="cite_ref-62" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-62" id="mwAk0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAk4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAk8">[</span>61<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAlE">By May 2014, Binay began his search for a running mate. As his potential running mate Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jinggoy_Estrada" id="mwAlI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jinggoy Estrada">Jinggoy Estrada</a> was in jail due to his implication in the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Priority_Development_Assistance_Fund_scam" id="mwAlM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Priority Development Assistance Fund scam">PDAF scam</a>,<sup about="#mwt256" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Macaraig2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Macaraig2-63"}}' id="cite_ref-Macaraig2_63-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Macaraig2-63" id="mwAlQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlY">[</span>62<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlc">]</span></span></a></sup> Binay's offers were declined by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JV_Ejercito" id="mwAlg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="JV Ejercito">JV Ejercito</a>,<sup about="#mwt259" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-64"}}' id="cite_ref-64" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-64" id="mwAlk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAls">[</span>63<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlw">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Villar" id="mwAl0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Villar">Manny Villar</a> (via wife <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cynthia_Villar" id="mwAl4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cynthia Villar">Cynthia Villar</a>),<sup about="#mwt262" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-65"}}' id="cite_ref-65" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-65" id="mwAl8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAmA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmE">[</span>64<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmI">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilma_Santos" id="mwAmM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vilma Santos">Vilma Santos</a> (via husband <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ralph_Recto" id="mwAmQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ralph Recto">Ralph Recto</a>),<sup about="#mwt265" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-66"}}' id="cite_ref-66" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-66" id="mwAmU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAmY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmc">[</span>65<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmg">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwAmk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a>,<sup about="#mwt268" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-67"}}' id="cite_ref-67" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-67" id="mwAmo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAms"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmw">[</span>66<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAm0">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwAm4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>,<sup about="#mwt271" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-68"}}' id="cite_ref-68" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-68" id="mwAm8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAnA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnE">[</span>67<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnI">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwAnM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a><sup about="#mwt272" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"duterte may232"}}' id="cite_ref-duterte_may232_69-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-duterte_may232-69" id="mwAnQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAnU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnY">[</span>68<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnc">]</span></span></a></sup> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Estrada" id="mwAng" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Joseph Estrada">Joseph Estrada</a>.<sup about="#mwt275" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-70"}}' id="cite_ref-70" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-70" id="mwAnk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAno"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAns">[</span>69<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnw">]</span></span></a></sup> Binay's daughter Abigail, also the Representative from Makati, said that Binay would accept anyone as his running mate except for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes" id="mwAn0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes">Antonio Trillanes</a>, and that she preferred <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwAn4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>, however Sen. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAn8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> was the most preferred by UNA officials to be his running mate.<sup about="#mwt278" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-71"}}' id="cite_ref-71" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-71" id="mwAoA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAoE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoI">[</span>70<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoM">]</span></span></a></sup> On June 12, Independence Day, speech in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo" id="mwAoQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo">Iloilo</a>, President Aquino said that he could only offer Binay the conduct of a clean and honest election, but not an outright endorsement.<sup about="#mwt281" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-72"}}' id="cite_ref-72" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-72" id="mwAoU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAoY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoc">[</span>71<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAog">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAok">On June 22, Binay resigned from the Aquino cabinet, both as presidential adviser on <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Overseas_Filipino_Workers" id="mwAoo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Overseas Filipino Workers">Overseas Filipino Workers</a>' concerns and as chairman of the Housing and Urban Development Coordinating Council, but did not say why.<sup about="#mwt284" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-73"}}' id="cite_ref-73" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-73" id="mwAos"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAow"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAo0">[</span>72<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAo4">]</span></span></a></sup> Two days later, Binay addressed the public from his Coconut Palace office, branding the current administration as "manhid at palpak" (insensitive and bumbling), but did not mention Aquino by name.<sup about="#mwt287" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-74"}}' id="cite_ref-74" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-74" id="mwAo8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwApA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwApE">[</span>73<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwApI">]</span></span></a></sup> His running mate was confirmed to be <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gregorio_Honasan" id="mwApM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gregorio Honasan">Gregorio Honasan</a>.</p>
<section aria-labelledby="Gringo_Honasan_(UNA)" data-mw-section-id="5" id="mwApQ"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Gringo_Honasan_(UNA)"><span id="Gringo_Honasan_.28UNA.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Gringo Honasan (UNA)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=5" title="Edit section: Gringo Honasan (UNA)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwApU">Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gregorio_Honasan" id="mwApY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gregorio Honasan">Gregorio Honasan</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nationalist_Alliance" id="mwApc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="United Nationalist Alliance">United Nationalist Alliance</a>) denied that he was going to be <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwApg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a>'s running mate in September 2015.<sup about="#mwt290" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-75"}}' id="cite_ref-75" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-75" id="mwApk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwApo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAps">[</span>74<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwApw">]</span></span></a></sup> By the next month, however, he said that his team-up with Binay was possible.<sup about="#mwt293" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-76"}}' id="cite_ref-76" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-76" id="mwAp0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAp4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAp8">[</span>75<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqA">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 12, 2015, Honasan announced that he will be running with Binay as the latter's vice president.<sup about="#mwt296" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Mallari-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Mallari-2015b-77"}}' id="cite_ref-Mallari-2015b_77-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Mallari-2015b-77" id="mwAqE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAqI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqM">[</span>76<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Miriam_Defensor_Santiago_(PRP)" data-mw-section-id="6" id="mwAqU"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Miriam_Defensor_Santiago_(PRP)"><span id="Miriam_Defensor_Santiago_.28PRP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Miriam Defensor Santiago (PRP)</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=6" title="Edit section: Miriam Defensor Santiago (PRP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt297" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Miriam Defensor Santiago 2016 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAqY" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt298" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt297" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwAqc" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago_2016_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago 2016 presidential campaign">Miriam Defensor Santiago 2016 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwAqg">In a press conference on July 2, 2014, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago" id="mwAqk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago">Miriam Defensor Santiago</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People's_Reform_Party" id="mwAqo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="People's Reform Party">People's Reform Party</a>) revealed that she was suffering from stage 4 <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lung_cancer" id="mwAqs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lung cancer">lung cancer</a> and she might run as president for the third time if it goes into remission.<sup about="#mwt301" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-78"}}' id="cite_ref-78" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-78" id="mwAqw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAq0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAq4">[</span>77<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAq8">]</span></span></a></sup> Called "the Iron Lady of Asia" and a recipient of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramon_Magsaysay_Award" id="mwArA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ramon Magsaysay Award">Ramon Magsaysay Award</a>, she was the widely expected winner of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwArE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">1992 election</a>, but lost after an inexplicably unscheduled <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_outage" id="mwArI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Power outage">power outage</a> during the counting of votes. She then ran for a Senate seat <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1995_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwArM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1995 Philippine Senate election">in 1995 and won</a>. She became one of the most popular senators since then, owing to her broad media visibility as well as to the high number of laws and bills she authored, a record that stands up to this day. She once again ran for president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1998_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwArQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1998 Philippine presidential election">in 1998, but lost</a>, citing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_propaganda" id="mwArU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Black propaganda">black propaganda</a> against her during the campaign.</p>
<p id="mwArY">By November 2014, Santiago, in a tweet, announced that "In the 2016 presidential elections, when I am rid of my lung cancer, I intend to claim the presidency I won in 1992." In a letter to the Senate, Santiago said that more than 90% of the cancer cells have regressed.<sup about="#mwt304" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-79"}}' id="cite_ref-79" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-79" id="mwArc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwArg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwArk">[</span>78<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAro">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwArs">Santiago announced her candidacy for president in the launch of her book "<i id="mwArw">Stupid is Forever</i>" on October 13, 2015. She ran under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People's_Reform_Party" id="mwAr0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="People's Reform Party">People's Reform Party</a>, the same party in which she ran under during her 1992 and 1998 presidential campaigns.<sup about="#mwt307" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Bartolome-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Bartolome-2015b-80"}}' id="cite_ref-Bartolome-2015b_80-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Bartolome-2015b-80" id="mwAr4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAr8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAsA">[</span>79<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAsE">]</span></span></a></sup> Days later, Santiago announced that Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAsI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> would be her vice presidential running mate, a decision that largely earned criticism from progressives as Marcos is the son of the former president and dictator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwAsM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">Ferdinand Marcos</a>. Her platform stood on the effective and efficient upholding and implementation of national and state-recognized international laws, enhancement of the agriculture industry with a focus on irrigation and farm-to-market roads, enhancing all seaports and airports in the country, creating at least one major government project in all provinces and all regions in the Philippines, building and highlighting the country's diplomatic affairs in the international level while skyrocketing the country's defense capabilities, and eradicating graft and corruption, which she believes to be the cause of poverty and inefficiency in the country.<sup about="#mwt310" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Hegina-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Hegina-2015b-81"}}' id="cite_ref-Hegina-2015b_81-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Hegina-2015b-81" id="mwAsQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAsU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAsY">[</span>80<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAsc">]</span></span></a></sup> Presidential candidate Rodrigo Duterte gave in an interview in November 2015 his comments on his fellow candidates' qualities, stating that "[i]f you want extraordinary competence and integrity, vote for Miriam."<sup about="#mwt313" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"no gasoline2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-no_gasoline2-82"}}' id="cite_ref-no_gasoline2_82-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-no_gasoline2-82" id="mwAsg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAsk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAso">[</span>81<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAss">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<section aria-labelledby="Bongbong_Marcos_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="7" id="mwAsw"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Bongbong_Marcos_(Independent)"><span id="Bongbong_Marcos_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Bongbong Marcos (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=7" title="Edit section: Bongbong Marcos (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwAs0">Asked on a November 2012 interview at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DZBB-AM" id="mwAs4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="DZBB-AM">DZBB</a> on his plans to run in 2016, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAs8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwAtA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>) said "never say never."<sup about="#mwt316" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-83"}}' id="cite_ref-83" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-83" id="mwAtE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAtI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAtM">[</span>82<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAtQ">]</span></span></a></sup> By March 2015, in another interview, this time at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DZMM" id="mwAtU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="DZMM">DZMM</a>, he said that he "will thoroughly study everything."<sup about="#mwt319" class="mw-ref reference"
