data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-84"}}' id="cite_ref-84" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-84" id="mwAtY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAtc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAtg">[</span>83<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAtk">]</span></span></a></sup> In August 2015, after reports surfaced of him being <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwAto" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a>'s running mate, Marcos clarified on an interview at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABS-CBN_News_Channel" id="mwAts" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="ABS-CBN News Channel">ABS-CBN News Channel</a> that he is still undecided on what position to run for, but he would certainly not be defending his Senate seat.<sup about="#mwt322" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-85"}}' id="cite_ref-85" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-85" id="mwAtw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAt0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAt4">[</span>84<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAt8">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 5, 2015, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAuA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> announced his candidacy for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vice_President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAuE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vice President of the Philippines">vice president</a> in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_general_election" id="mwAuI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine general election">2016 election</a>, and would support the candidacy of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwAuM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> if he ever does run.<sup about="#mwt325" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"CNN-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-CNN-2015b-86"}}' id="cite_ref-CNN-2015b_86-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-CNN-2015b-86" id="mwAuQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAuU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAuY">[</span>85<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAuc">]</span></span></a></sup> Days later, Duterte declined Cayetano's and Marcos' offers to be their presidential running mate.</p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Rodrigo_Duterte_(PDP–Laban)" data-mw-section-id="8" id="mwAug"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Rodrigo_Duterte_(PDP–Laban)"><span id="Rodrigo_Duterte_.28PDP.E2.80.93Laban.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Rodrigo Duterte (PDP–Laban)</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=8" title="Edit section: Rodrigo Duterte (PDP–Laban)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt326" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Rodrigo Duterte 2016 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAuk" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt327" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt326" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwAuo" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte_2016_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte 2016 presidential campaign">Rodrigo Duterte 2016 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwAus">Early in 2015, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwAuw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> Mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwAu0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> made hints to the media of his intent to run for the presidency come 2016, with a widely praised promise of abolishing Congress altogether in favor of a Parliament should he win.<sup about="#mwt330" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-87"}}' id="cite_ref-87" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-87" id="mwAu4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAu8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvA">[</span>86<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt333" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-88"}}' id="cite_ref-88" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-88" id="mwAvI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAvM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvQ">[</span>87<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvU">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt336" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-89"}}' id="cite_ref-89" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-89" id="mwAvY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAvc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvg">[</span>88<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAvk">]</span></span></a></sup> Earlier, in February 2014, Duterte was reportedly enjoying the support of several <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Netizen" id="mwAvo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Netizen">netizens</a> lauding his performance as mayor of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwAvs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a>, especially in maintaining peace and order in the city, but he was quick to shrug off calls for him to run for president, saying he was not qualified for a higher public office.<sup about="#mwt339" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-90"}}' id="cite_ref-90" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-90" id="mwAvw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAv0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAv4">[</span>89<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAv8">]</span></span></a></sup> A year later, Duterte said in a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baguio" id="mwAwA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Baguio">Baguio</a> federalism forum that he'll only run for president "if (it is) to save the republic." Duterte cited the need of about 10 to 15 billion pesos for a campaign war chest as what was keeping him from running.<sup about="#mwt342" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-91"}}' id="cite_ref-91" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-91" id="mwAwE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAwI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAwM">[</span>90<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAwQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Days later, however, Duterte "re-entered" <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP–Laban" id="mwAwU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PDP–Laban">PDP–Laban</a>; he maintained he never left the party, and only had to stand under a local party banner (<i id="mwAwY">Hugpong</i>) in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Philippine_general_election" id="mwAwc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Philippine general election">2013 local election</a> in order to ensure his victory.<sup about="#mwt345" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-92"}}' id="cite_ref-92" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-92" id="mwAwg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAwk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAwo">[</span>91<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAws">]</span></span></a></sup> PDP–Laban president <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aquilino_Pimentel_III" id="mwAww" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aquilino Pimentel III">Aquilino Pimentel III</a> later said that Duterte was among his party's options for a presidential candidate for 2016, noting that the party's position on <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federalism" id="mwAw0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Federalism">federalism</a> coincides with Duterte's advocacy.<sup about="#mwt348" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-93"}}' id="cite_ref-93" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-93" id="mwAw4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAw8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAxA">[</span>92<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAxE">]</span></span></a></sup> A few days after reports came out saying incumbent Vice President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwAxI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a> (a presidential aspirant) was eyeing Duterte as his possible running mate, Duterte issued a statement saying he was not interested in running for a national post.<sup about="#mwt351" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"duterte may232"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-duterte_may232-69"}}' id="cite_ref-duterte_may232_69-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-duterte_may232-69" id="mwAxM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAxQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAxU">[</span>68<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAxY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAxc">In September 2014, Duterte already declined presidential aspirant and incumbent senator <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor-Santiago" id="mwAxg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor-Santiago">Miriam Defensor-Santiago</a>'s offer to be her running mate, saying that instead of picking him as the senator's running mate, she could ask former defense secretary <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilberto_Teodoro,_Jr." id="mwAxk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gilberto Teodoro, Jr.">Gilberto Teodoro, Jr.</a> instead.<sup about="#mwt354" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-94"}}' id="cite_ref-94" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-94" id="mwAxo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAxs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAxw">[</span>93<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAx0">]</span></span></a></sup> However, in October 2015, Santiago chose <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAx4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> as her running mate.<sup about="#mwt357" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-95"}}' id="cite_ref-95" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-95" id="mwAx8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAyA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAyE">[</span>94<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAyI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAyM">Later, during the June 21, 2015 airing of his weekly program in a local channel (<i id="mwAyQ">Gikan Sa Masa, Para Sa Masa</i>), Duterte stated that he was considering suggestions from his friends and supporters to run for president. He also added that he will stop expressing non-interest in starting a presidential campaign.<sup about="#mwt360" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-96"}}' id="cite_ref-96" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-96" id="mwAyU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAyY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAyc">[</span>95<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAyg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAyk">On September 7, 2015, in a press conference held at Davao City, Duterte officially declared he will not be running for president in 2016 and apologized to all of his supporters on the decision. He additionally stated that he might retire from politics after his term as mayor of Davao City ends in 2016 and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwAyo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">his daughter</a> runs for the office. Other factors he states of his possible retirement included age, health concerns and his family's objections.<sup about="#mwt361" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-97"}}' id="cite_ref-97" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-97" id="mwAys"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAyw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAy0">[</span>96<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAy4">]</span></span></a></sup> Mixed reactions erupted in social media hours after the declaration and several supporters still continued to petition online, urging the mayor to revert his decision.<sup about="#mwt362" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-98"}}' id="cite_ref-98" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-98" id="mwAy8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAzA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAzE">[</span>97<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAzI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAzM">On September 26, 2015, a large number of supporters gathered at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quirino_Grandstand" id="mwAzQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quirino Grandstand">Quirino Grandstand</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rizal_Park" id="mwAzU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rizal Park">Rizal Park</a>, Manila to urge Duterte to officially run for the presidency in 2016.<sup about="#mwt363" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-99"}}' id="cite_ref-99" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-99" id="mwAzY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAzc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAzg">[</span>98<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAzk">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt364" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-100"}}' id="cite_ref-100" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-100" id="mwAzo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAzs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAzw">[</span>99<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAz0">]</span></span></a></sup> Despite being an undeclared candidate, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse_Asia" id="mwAz4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pulse Asia">Pulse Asia</a> Research Director Ana Tabunda still considered Duterte "a serious contender" as he was already ranked 4th in a presidential survey done by Pulse Asia from late August to early September, as well as in a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_Weather_Stations" id="mwAz8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Social Weather Stations">Social Weather Stations</a> survey.<sup about="#mwt365" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-101"}}' id="cite_ref-101" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-101" id="mwA0A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA0E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA0I">[</span>100<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA0M">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte's long-time political rival and critic, former <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Philippine_House_of_Representatives" id="mwA0Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Speaker of the Philippine House of Representatives">House Speaker</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prospero_Nograles" id="mwA0U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Prospero Nograles">Prospero Nograles</a>, also expressed his support for Duterte if ever the latter decided to run for the presidency.<sup about="#mwt368" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-102"}}' id="cite_ref-102" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-102" id="mwA0Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA0c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA0g">[</span>101<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA0k">]</span></span></a></sup> On September 29, incumbent Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwA0o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> declared his intention to run for the 2016 vice presidential post in a press event held in Davao City and considered Duterte his first choice as running mate for the presidential post; this eventually led to a meeting between the two parties.<sup about="#mwt369" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-103"}}' id="cite_ref-103" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-103" id="mwA0s"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA0w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA00">[</span>102<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA04">]</span></span></a></sup> A day after meeting with Cayetano, Duterte met with another incumbent senator, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwA08" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>, who also went to Davao City to personally talk to Duterte. Marcos was reportedly considering running for the vice presidential post as well; Marcos had not yet made any formal proclamation at that time. As with the Cayetano meeting, Duterte still declared that he was not running for president.<sup about="#mwt372" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-104"}}' id="cite_ref-104" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-104" id="mwA1A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA1E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA1I">[</span>103<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA1M">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA1Q">On October 13, 2015, in a press conference held at a local hotel in Davao City, Duterte finalized his decision not to run for the presidency, with daughter <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwA1U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara</a>'s objection being pointed out as one of the main contributors.<sup about="#mwt373" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-105"}}' id="cite_ref-105" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-105" id="mwA1Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA1c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA1g">[</span>104<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA1k">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA1o">On October 15, 2015, Duterte, through a representative, filed his candidacy for Mayor of Davao City at the local elections office.<sup about="#mwt376" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-106"}}' id="cite_ref-106" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-106" id="mwA1s"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA1w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA10">[</span>105<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA14">]</span></span></a></sup> A day later, Duterte's daughter Sara, posted on her <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Instagram" id="mwA18" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Instagram">Instagram</a> account a photo of her candidacy as mayor of Davao City, while hinting that her father would seek a higher post and withdraw his mayoral re-election bid.<sup about="#mwt377" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-107"}}' id="cite_ref-107" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-107" id="mwA2A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA2E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA2I">[</span>106<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA2M">]</span></span></a></sup> However, it was not received by the commission in Davao City.<sup about="#mwt378" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-108"}}' id="cite_ref-108" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-108" id="mwA2Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA2U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA2Y">[</span>107<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA2c">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte's possibility of substitution until December 10 was opened after <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martin_Diño" id="mwA2g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Martin Diño">Martin Diño</a>, father of celebrity <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liza_Diño" id="mwA2k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liza Diño">Liza Diño</a>, chairman of Volunteers Against Crime and Corruption, and former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barangay_captain" id="mwA2o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Barangay captain">barangay captain</a> of Brgy. San Antonio, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwA2s" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>, filed his candidacy for president on the last minute. Diño is a member of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP–Laban" id="mwA2w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PDP–Laban">PDP–Laban</a>, the same party advocating the federalism system where Duterte belongs.<sup about="#mwt379" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-109"}}' id="cite_ref-109" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-109" id="mwA20"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA24"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA28">[</span>108<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA3A">]</span></span></a></sup> Diño clarified that there was a "clerical error" on his submitted candidacy (running for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasay" id="mwA3E" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasay">Pasay</a> mayor, instead of president). On their general meeting held in Pasay on October 26, members of PDP–Laban had expressed their support to Diño in his presidential bid.<sup about="#mwt382" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-110"}}' id="cite_ref-110" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-110" id="mwA3I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA3M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA3Q">[</span>109<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA3U">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 21, Duterte told <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNN_Philippines" id="mwA3Y" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="CNN Philippines">CNN Philippines</a>' <i id="mwA3c">News.PH</i> interview that there was still a chance he would change his mind. The decision, however, would have to be made by the PDP–Laban.<sup about="#mwt385" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-111"}}' id="cite_ref-111" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-111" id="mwA3g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA3k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA3o">[</span>110<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA3s">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA3w">On October 27, PDP–Laban confirmed that Duterte will substitute as the party's presidential bet if Diño withdraws or is disqualified by the Commission of Elections.<sup about="#mwt388" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-112"}}' id="cite_ref-112" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-112" id="mwA30"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA34"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA38">[</span>111<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA4A">]</span></span></a></sup> Two days later, Diño withdrew his presidential bid and named Duterte as his substitute because of a possibility that Diño might be declared as a nuisance candidate by the commission.<sup about="#mwt391" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-113"}}' id="cite_ref-113" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-113" id="mwA4E"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA4I"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA4M">[</span>112<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA4Q">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA4U">On November 21, in a gathering held in his alma mater <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Beda_College" id="mwA4Y" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="San Beda College">San Beda College</a>, Duterte formally announced his presidential bid and also finally accepted <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwA4c" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a>'s offer to be his running mate.<sup about="#mwt394" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-114"}}' id="cite_ref-114" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-114" id="mwA4g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA4k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA4o">[</span>113<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA4s">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte said he was disappointed over the decision made by the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Senate_Electoral_Tribunal" id="mwA4w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Senate Electoral Tribunal">Senate Electoral Tribunal</a> regarding <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwA40" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>'s citizenship as well as the current administration's handling of the '<a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015_Ninoy_Aquino_International_Airport_bullet_planting_scandal" id="mwA44" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2015 Ninoy Aquino International Airport bullet planting scandal">laglag-bala</a>' issue.<sup about="#mwt397" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-115"}}' id="cite_ref-115" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-115" id="mwA48"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA5A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA5E">[</span>114<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA5I">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte further stated that he will file his candidacy immediately after he reached out to his party.<sup about="#mwt398" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-116"}}' id="cite_ref-116" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-116" id="mwA5M"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA5Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA5U">[</span>115<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA5Y">]</span></span></a></sup> However, the legality of Duterte's substitution for Diño was questioned by some electoral lawyers because of an error made in Diño's certificate of candidacy that made him a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasay" id="mwA5c" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasay">Pasay</a> mayoral bet despite being a resident of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwA5g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>.</p>
<p id="mwA5k">Duterte topped all other candidates, including former front-runner <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwA5o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>, in a survey published November 25, 2015, and held by Pulse Asia.<sup about="#mwt401" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-117"}}' id="cite_ref-117" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-117" id="mwA5s"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA5w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA50">[</span>116<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA54">]</span></span></a></sup> Poe said that the survey was 'inconclusive' and 'not reflective' and claimed it was made by Duterte's camp.<sup about="#mwt404" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-118"}}' id="cite_ref-118" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-118" id="mwA58"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA6A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA6E">[</span>117<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA6I">]</span></span></a></sup> The day after, Duterte filed his certificate of candidacy at the commission main office in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila" id="mwA6M" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila">Manila</a> and withdrew his bid for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_Davao_City" id="mwA6Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mayor of Davao City">Mayor of Davao City</a> (for which he was substituted by his daughter, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwA6U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara</a>).<sup about="#mwt407" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-119"}}' id="cite_ref-119" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-119" id="mwA6Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA6c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA6g">[</span>118<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA6k">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<section aria-labelledby="Alan_Peter_Cayetano_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="9" id="mwA6o"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Alan_Peter_Cayetano_(Independent)"><span id="Alan_Peter_Cayetano_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Alan Peter Cayetano (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=9" title="Edit section: Alan Peter Cayetano (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwA6s">In a March 2013 interview with the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABS-CBN_News_Channel" id="mwA6w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="ABS-CBN News Channel">ABS-CBN News Channel</a>, Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwA60" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwA64" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>), said that "I want to be president of this country."<sup about="#mwt410" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-120"}}' id="cite_ref-120" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-120" id="mwA68"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA7A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA7E">[</span>119<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA7I">]</span></span></a></sup> In September 2015, after several surveys saw him trailing in the presidential race, said that he is withdrawing from the presidential election, and would instead focus for the vice presidency, preferably as the running mate of either <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwA7M" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a> or <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwA7Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a>.</p>
<p id="mwA7U">A few days later in a Davao City meeting, Cayetano announced that he will seek candidacy for running as vice president. Cayetano, who he is the member of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwA7Y" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>, did not mention his presidential running-mate as the Nacionalistas will meet in a few days, for their decision.<sup about="#mwt413" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-121"}}' id="cite_ref-121" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-121" id="mwA7c"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA7g"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA7k">[</span>120<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA7o">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 1, Duterte said that if he would ever run for president, he would prefer Cayetano to be his running mate.<sup about="#mwt416" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-122"}}' id="cite_ref-122" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-122" id="mwA7s"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA7w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA70">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA74">]</span></span></a></sup> Days later, Duterte declined the offer to be Cayetano and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwA78" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>' offers to be their presidential running mate.<sup about="#mwt419" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-123"}}' id="cite_ref-123" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-123" id="mwA8A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA8E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA8I">[</span>122<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA8M">]</span></span></a></sup> However, during a phone interview with the <i id="mwA8Q"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Daily_Inquirer" id="mwA8U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Daily Inquirer">Philippine Daily Inquirer</a></i> In November 2015, Cayetano confirmed Duterte as his running mate if ever the latter confirms his candidacy for presidency.<sup about="#mwt422" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-124"}}' id="cite_ref-124" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-124" id="mwA8Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA8c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA8g">[</span>123<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA8k">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Grace_Poe_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="10" id="mwA8o"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Grace_Poe_(Independent)"><span id="Grace_Poe_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Grace Poe (Independent)</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=10" title="Edit section: Grace Poe (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt423" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Grace Poe 2016 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwA8s" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt424" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt423" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwA8w" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe_2016_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe 2016 presidential campaign">Grace Poe 2016 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwA80"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwA84" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>'s surprising first-place finish in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwA88" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Philippine Senate election">2013 Senate election</a> as an independent made her a likely contender for the presidency but she dismissed any plans of running in April 2014, saying she was not considering "anything higher at this point" and even declaring that Senator Miriam Defensor Santiago was the most qualified to run as president.<sup about="#mwt427" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-125"}}' id="cite_ref-125" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-125" id="mwA9A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA9E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9I">[</span>124<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9M">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA9Q">On <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_presidential_trips_made_by_Benigno_Aquino_III#.C2.A0United_States_and_.C2.A0Canada_.28May_6_to_9.2C_2015.29" id="mwA9U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="List of presidential trips made by Benigno Aquino III">President Aquino's state visit to Canada in May 2015</a>, the president disclosed that he had met with Poe prior to the trip, although he didn't say what the meeting was for.<sup about="#mwt430" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-126"}}' id="cite_ref-126" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-126" id="mwA9Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA9c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9g">[</span>125<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9k">]</span></span></a></sup> A couple of days later, Poe confirmed that she did meet with Aquino. Poe said that they "discussed his intention to choose a candidate who, first and foremost, has the trust of the nation, and, secondly, has the potential to win in the election, in order to sustain the reforms especially against corruption and the pro-poor programs of the government." Poe expected more meetings with Aquino in June.<sup about="#mwt433" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-127"}}' id="cite_ref-127" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-127" id="mwA9o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA9s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9w">[</span>126<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA90">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA94">On June 2, UNA interim president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toby_Tiangco" id="mwA98" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Toby Tiangco">Toby Tiangco</a>, responding to calls for Jejomar Binay to "come clean" on his corruption allegations, said in a press conference that Poe is not qualified to run either for president or vice president after citing her certificate of candidacy in the 2013 Senate election, which stated that she is a resident for six years and six months; adding three years for the 2016 election, nine years and six months or six months short mandated by the constitution.<sup about="#mwt436" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-128"}}' id="cite_ref-128" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-128" id="mwA-A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-I">[</span>127<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-M">]</span></span></a></sup> Two days later, before a Senate session, Poe said that she wrote "six years and six months" because it was in April 2006 that her home in the United States was sold. Poe, who had been a resident of the United States for 13 years, returned to the Philippines after her father <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fernando_Poe,_Jr." id="mwA-Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Fernando Poe, Jr.">Fernando Poe, Jr.</a>, a <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fernando_Poe,_Jr._presidential_campaign,_2004" id="mwA-U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Fernando Poe, Jr. presidential campaign, 2004">presidential candidate in the 2004 elections</a>, died in December 2004. She said that she had proof that she has been living in the Philippines since February 2005. She said, that despite being a congressman for Navotas, Tiangco lives elsewhere, and that her decision on whether to run in 2016 is "50%" sure. Poe also observed that the attacks from UNA only began after she signed the Senate Blue Ribbon subcommittee report recommending plunder and graft cases against Binay.<sup about="#mwt439" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-129"}}' id="cite_ref-129" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-129" id="mwA-Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-g">[</span>128<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-k">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA-o">On September 16, at a gathering at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_the_Philippines_Diliman" id="mwA-s" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="University of the Philippines Diliman">University of the Philippines Diliman</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwA-w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>, Poe announced her intention to seek the presidency, saying that "no one person or group has a monopoly on a straight path advocacy" of President Aquino, a shot against the Aquino party's nominee Roxas, who was advocating for continuation of the "Daang Matuwid" (straight path) advocacy of Aquino; while also hitting the Administration programs. This resulted in the Palace questioning their Daang Matuwid advocacy contrary to their speeches.<sup about="#mwt442" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-130"}}' id="cite_ref-130" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-130" id="mwA-0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-8">[</span>129<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_A">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA_E">On December 1, the commission's second division had formally disqualified Poe from running as president in the 2016 elections and cancelled her filed Certificate of Candidacy for not failing to meet with citizenship and residency requirements. The division voted 3–0 in favor of the petition filed by Attorney Estrella Elamparo to disqualify Poe. The decision stated that Poe had failed to comply with the 10-year residency requirement, mandatory for a presidential candidate.<sup about="#mwt445" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-131"}}' id="cite_ref-131" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-131" id="mwA_I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_Q">[</span>130<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_U">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA_Y">On December 23, 2015, the commission <i id="mwA_c">en banc</i> disqualified Poe from running as president in the 2016 elections for failing to meet the 10-year residency requirement.<sup about="#mwt448" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-132"}}' id="cite_ref-132" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-132" id="mwA_g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_o">[</span>131<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_s">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt449" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-133"}}' id="cite_ref-133" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-133" id="mwA_w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_4">[</span>132<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_8">]</span></span></a></sup> Poe said she would appeal the disqualification to the Supreme Court. On December 28, 2015, the Supreme Court issued two <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporary_restraining_order" id="mwBAA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Temporary restraining order">temporary restraining orders</a> against the decision of the commission <i id="mwBAE">en banc</i>.<sup about="#mwt450" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-134"}}' id="cite_ref-134" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-134" id="mwBAI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBAM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAQ">[</span>133<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBAY">On March 8, 2016, voting 9–6, the Supreme Court voted to affirm Poe's <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_nationality_law" id="mwBAc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine nationality law">natural-born status</a> and 10-year residency.<sup about="#mwt453" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"naturally2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-naturally2-135"}}' id="cite_ref-naturally2_135-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-naturally2-135" id="mwBAg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBAk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAo">[</span>134<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAs">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt456" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"truly2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-truly2-136"}}' id="cite_ref-truly2_136-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-truly2-136" id="mwBAw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBA0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBA4">[</span>135<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBA8">]</span></span></a></sup> On April 9, 2016, the Supreme Court declared their ruling as final and executory.<sup about="#mwt459" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-137"}}' id="cite_ref-137" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-137" id="mwBBA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBBE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBI">[</span>136<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<section aria-labelledby="Francis_Escudero_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="11" id="mwBBQ"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Francis_Escudero_(Independent)"><span id="Francis_Escudero_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Francis Escudero (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=11" title="Edit section: Francis Escudero (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBBU">Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Escudero" id="mwBBY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Escudero">Francis Escudero</a> (independent) said in a March 2012 <i id="mwBBc"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rappler" id="mwBBg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rappler">Rappler</a></i> interview that "Let me be honest, candid, yet factual about it. I would be very interested in seeking a higher office in 2016 for the simple reason that I'm halfway through my last term."<sup about="#mwt462" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-138"}}' id="cite_ref-138" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-138" id="mwBBk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBBo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBs">[</span>137<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBw">]</span></span></a></sup> By May 2015, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwBB0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a> told reporters that she would not run against Escudero.<sup about="#mwt465" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-139"}}' id="cite_ref-139" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-139" id="mwBB4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBB8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCA">[</span>138<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBCI">On September 17 at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Club_Filipino" id="mwBCM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Club Filipino">Club Filipino</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Juan,_Metro_Manila" id="mwBCQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="San Juan, Metro Manila">San Juan</a>, Escudero announced his vice presidential bid, becoming Poe's running mate.<sup about="#mwt468" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Ager-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Ager-2015b-140"}}' id="cite_ref-Ager-2015b_140-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Ager-2015b-140" id="mwBCU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBCY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCc">[</span>139<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Mar_Roxas_(Liberal)" data-mw-section-id="12" id="mwBCk"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Mar_Roxas_(Liberal)"><span id="Mar_Roxas_.28Liberal.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Mar Roxas (Liberal)</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=12" title="Edit section: Mar Roxas (Liberal)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt469" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Mar Roxas 2016 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBCo" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt470" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt469" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBCs" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas_2016_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas 2016 presidential campaign">Mar Roxas 2016 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBCw">Senate President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Franklin_Drilon" id="mwBC0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Franklin Drilon">Franklin Drilon</a>, when describing the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwBC4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a>'s plans for Interior Secretary <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwBC8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a>' in 2016, told the media in January 2013 that "so far as the LP is concerned, [and] in so far as I am concerned, we believe that he is best qualified for 2016."<sup about="#mwt473" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-141"}}' id="cite_ref-141" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-141" id="mwBDA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDI">[</span>140<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDM">]</span></span></a></sup> Two years later, Drilon told <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DZIQ" id="mwBDQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="DZIQ">DZIQ</a> AM radio that Roxas had expressed his interest internally within the party.<sup about="#mwt476" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-142"}}' id="cite_ref-142" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-142" id="mwBDU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDc">[</span>141<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDg">]</span></span></a></sup> Several Liberal Party stalwarts had by then expressed that Roxas should declare his intentions at that time,<sup about="#mwt479" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-143"}}' id="cite_ref-143" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-143" id="mwBDk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDs">[</span>142<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDw">]</span></span></a></sup> with some such as Budget Secretary <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Florencio_Abad" id="mwBD0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Florencio Abad">Florencio Abad</a> suggesting that Roxas may slide down to run for the vice presidency again.<sup about="#mwt482" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-144"}}' id="cite_ref-144" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-144" id="mwBD4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBD8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEA">[</span>143<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBEI">Aquino had a series of meetings between Roxas, Grace Poe and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Escudero" id="mwBEM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Escudero">Francis Escudero</a> from prior to Aquino's state visit to Canada in May, until days before his final <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_of_the_Nation_Address_(Philippines)" id="mwBEQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="State of the Nation Address (Philippines)">State of the Nation Address</a> in July, including a July dinner with all three of them at the Bahay Pangarap, Aquino's official residence at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malacañang_Palace" id="mwBEU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Malacañang Palace">Malacañang Palace</a> complex. While Roxas was seen as Aquino's choice to succeed him, another question was who would be Roxas' running mate, as Poe had earlier said that she'd rather run with Escudero as her running mate.<sup about="#mwt485" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-145"}}' id="cite_ref-145" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-145" id="mwBEY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBEc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEg">[</span>144<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBEo">On July 31, 2015, at an event dubbed as "A Gathering of Friends", Roxas formally accepted the Liberal Party's nomination after he was officially endorsed by President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwBEs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a> in the presence of their political allies at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Club_Filipino" id="mwBEw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Club Filipino">Club Filipino</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Juan,_Metro_Manila" id="mwBE0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="San Juan, Metro Manila">San Juan</a>, where Roxas had announced his decision to withdraw from the 2010 presidential election and give way to Aquino's presidential bid. Aquino also announced his candidacy there on September 9, 2009.<sup about="#mwt488" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Alvarez-2015c"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Alvarez-2015c-146"}}' id="cite_ref-Alvarez-2015c_146-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Alvarez-2015c-146" id="mwBE4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBE8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFA">[</span>145<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFE">]</span></span></a></sup> On the same day, Roxas formally launched his campaign website.</p>
<section aria-labelledby="Leni_Robredo_(Liberal)" data-mw-section-id="13" id="mwBFI"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Leni_Robredo_(Liberal)"><span id="Leni_Robredo_.28Liberal.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Leni Robredo (Liberal)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=13" title="Edit section: Leni Robredo (Liberal)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBFM">In August 2015, Representative <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwBFQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a>, from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwBFU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>'s third district (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwBFY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a>), said that talk of plans of her running in 2016 is "too soon", and that she'd only run if she's "indispensable".<sup about="#mwt491" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-147"}}' id="cite_ref-147" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-147" id="mwBFc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBFg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFk">[</span>146<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBFs">After the initial objections of her daughters, Robredo decided to run for vice president, accepting the offers of President Aquino and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwBFw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a>.<sup about="#mwt494" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-148"}}' id="cite_ref-148" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-148" id="mwBF0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBF4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBF8">[</span>147<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGA">]</span></span></a></sup> She officially announced her candidacy for the office on October 5, 2015, at the Club Filipino.<sup about="#mwt497" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Cruz-2015b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Cruz-2015b-149"}}' id="cite_ref-Cruz-2015b_149-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Cruz-2015b-149" id="mwBGE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBGI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGM">[</span>148<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Standalone_vice_presidential_candidate" data-mw-section-id="14" id="mwBGU"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Standalone_vice_presidential_candidate">Standalone vice presidential candidate</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=14" title="Edit section: Standalone vice presidential candidate"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBGY">One vice presidential candidate did not have a running mate.</p>
<section aria-labelledby="Antonio_Trillanes_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="15" id="mwBGc"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Antonio_Trillanes_(Independent)"><span id="Antonio_Trillanes_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Antonio Trillanes (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=15" title="Edit section: Antonio Trillanes (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBGg">In the news program <i id="mwBGk"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bandila" id="mwBGo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bandila">Bandila</a>'</i>s May 30, 2014, segment where a guest is asked to answer only "yes" or "no," Senator <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes_IV" id="mwBGs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes IV">Antonio Trillanes IV</a> (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwBGw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>) was asked if he would run for vice president in 2016 and responded by saying "Let's just say yes, I will run. As for what position, I will abide by the Nacionalista Party."<sup about="#mwt500" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-150"}}' id="cite_ref-150" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-150" id="mwBG0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBG4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBG8">[</span>149<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBHE">By August 2015, Trillanes revealed his plans of running as an independent vice presidential candidate, saying that the Nacionalistas would not endorse a candidate if more than one of them ran for the vice presidency. Trillanes' own group, <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magdalo_para_sa_Pilipino" id="mwBHI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Magdalo para sa Pilipino">Magdalo</a>, backed his vice presidential bid.<sup about="#mwt503" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-151"}}' id="cite_ref-151" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-151" id="mwBHM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBHQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHU">[</span>150<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBHc">In a general assembly of MAGDALO coalition members, Trillanes announced that he will run for vice president as an Independent, but will support the presidential bid of Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwBHg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>.<sup about="#mwt506" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-152"}}' id="cite_ref-152" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-152" id="mwBHk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBHo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHs">[</span>151<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt509" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-153"}}' id="cite_ref-153" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-153" id="mwBH0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBH4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBH8">[</span>152<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBIE">Trillanes questioned the presidential bid of Rodrigo Duterte, saying that if Duterte wins, his presidency will be a disaster to the Philippines.<sup about="#mwt512" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-154"}}' id="cite_ref-154" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-154" id="mwBII"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBIM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIQ">[</span>153<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section></section><section aria-labelledby="Debates" data-mw-section-id="16" id="mwBIY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Debates">Debates</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=16" title="Edit section: Debates"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt513" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"PiliPinas Debates 2016"},"2":{"wt":"Harapan ng Bise"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBIc" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt514" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt513" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBIg" role="note">Main articles: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2016" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2016">PiliPinas Debates 2016</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harapan_ng_Bise" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Harapan ng Bise">Harapan ng Bise</a></div>
<p id="mwBIk">The Commission on Elections confirmed that they will organize three presidential debates and one vice presidential debate. This was the first time that the commission organized debates since the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwBIo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">1992 presidential election</a>. The debates were dubbed as <i id="mwBIs"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2016" id="mwBIw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2016">PiliPinas Debates 2016</a></i> (<abbr about="#mwt515" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwBI0\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"The Philippines Chooses"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBI4" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt515" id="mwBI8"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>The Philippines Chooses</span>).<sup about="#mwt519" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-155"}}' id="cite_ref-155" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-155" id="mwBJA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBJE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJI">[</span>154<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBJQ">A separate debate, <i id="mwBJU"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harapan_ng_Bise" id="mwBJY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Harapan ng Bise">Harapan ng Bise</a></i> (<abbr about="#mwt520" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwBJc\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Face-off of the Vice [Presidents]"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBJg" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt520" id="mwBJk"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Face-off of the Vice [Presidents]</span>), was organized and hosted by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABS-CBN" id="mwBJo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="ABS-CBN">ABS-CBN</a>. Not all vice presidential candidates attended.<sup about="#mwt524" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-156"}}' id="cite_ref-156" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-156" id="mwBJs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBJw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJ0">[</span>155<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJ4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<table class="wikitable" id="mwBJ8" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align:center;" width="75%">
<caption id="mwBKA">Legend</caption>
<tbody id="mwBKE"><tr id="mwBKI">
<td id="mwBKM"><span about="#mwt525" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Colors","href":"./Template:Colors"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"black"},"2":{"wt":"#90ff90"},"3":{"wt":"&amp;nbsp;P&amp;nbsp;"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBKQ" style="background-color:
#90ff90; color:black;" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>P<span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span> <span about="#mwt526" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"small","href":"./Template:Small"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Participated"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBKU" style="font-size: 85%;" typeof="mw:Transclusion">Participated</span> <span about="#mwt527" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Colors","href":"./Template:Colors"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"black"},"2":{"wt":"#ff9090"},"3":{"wt":"&amp;nbsp;A&amp;nbsp;"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBKY" style="background-color:
#ff9090; color:black;" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>A<span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span> <span about="#mwt528" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"small","href":"./Template:Small"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Absent"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBKc" style="font-size: 85%;" typeof="mw:Transclusion">Absent</span></td></tr>
</tbody></table>

</section><section aria-labelledby="Vice_presidential_debates" data-mw-section-id="18" id="mwBPM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Vice_presidential_debates">Vice presidential debates</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=18" title="Edit section: Vice presidential debates"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<table class="wikitable" id="mwBPQ">
<tbody id="mwBPU"><tr id="mwBPY"><th id="mwBPc">Date</th>
<th id="mwBPg">Organizers</th>
<th id="mwBPk">Media partners</th>
<th id="mwBPo">Location</th>
<th id="mwBPs" style="width:5em;">Cayetano</th>
<th id="mwBPw" style="width:5em;">Escudero</th>
<th id="mwBP0" style="width:5em;">Honasan</th>

</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Opinion_polling" data-mw-section-id="19" id="mwBTA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Opinion_polling">Opinion polling</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=19" title="Edit section: Opinion polling"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt529" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Opinion polling for the 2016 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBTE" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt530" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt529" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBTI" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_for_the_2016_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Opinion polling for the 2016 Philippine presidential election">Opinion polling for the 2016 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwBTM">Opinion polling in Philippines is conducted by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_Weather_Stations" id="mwBTQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Social Weather Stations">Social Weather Stations</a> (SWS), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse_Asia" id="mwBTU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pulse Asia">Pulse Asia</a> and other third-party pollsters.</p>
