</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Campaign" data-mw-section-id="22" id="mwBTw"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Campaign">Campaign</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=22" title="Edit section: Campaign"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwBT0" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png" id="mwBT4"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwBT8" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png/250px-Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png/500px-Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2016_Philippine_elections.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwBUA">Provinces that have more than one million voters for this election. Together, along with <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwBUE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a>, this comprises more than half of the registered voters for this election. Candidates usually personally campaign in these locales in order to reduce costs.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwBUI">While the campaign period as set by law started on February 9, 2016, candidates had earlier started campaigning as early as late 2015, as the courts have struck the ban on premature campaigning as unconstitutional.</p>
<section aria-labelledby="Issues" data-mw-section-id="23" id="mwBUM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Issues">Issues</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=23" title="Edit section: Issues"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<!-- Reminders on editing section:

1. There is currently one reference in this section. It's the Deutsche Welle (DW) news article: http://www.dw.com/en/who&#x2D;are&#x2D;the&#x2D;philippines&#x2D;top&#x2D;presidential&#x2D;candidates/a&#x2D;19035071
2. If you're adding something new, it's not on the DW reference, make sure that it's cited, and put it after (or before) the paragraph that cited by the DW reference. Don't put it in the middle.
3. If you're adding something new, it's not on the DW reference, and you don't intend to add a reference, don't do it. Please read WP:OR for details why.

Thanks!
-->
<p id="mwBUQ">The continuation of outgoing president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwBUU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a>'s policies is seen to be the main theme for this election. Former Interior Secretary <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwBUY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a> promises the continuation of the "Daang Matuwid" ("straight path") anti-corruption drive by the last president, which is appealing among the elites. Roxas's base was mostly middle-class Filipinos. Vice President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwBUc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a>'s policies is seen to be opposite of Aquino's, such as the former's opposition to the enactment of the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangsamoro_Basic_Law" id="mwBUg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bangsamoro Basic Law">Bangsamoro Basic Law</a>, and on how to approach <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China" id="mwBUk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="China">China</a> concerning the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Territorial_disputes_in_the_South_China_Sea" id="mwBUo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Territorial disputes in the South China Sea">territorial disputes in the South China Sea</a>, while presenting himself as a man of the people, and fighting out allegations of corruption while serving as mayor of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwBUs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a>, the country's financial hub. Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwBUw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a>'s campaign is seen to be an alternative to Roxas and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwBU0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwBU4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a>; her image as untarnished by corruption is liked by the elite, while she sends messages that are pro-poor. Duterte is banking on a law and order platform, which includes the proposals concerning <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federalism_in_the_Philippines" id="mwBU8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Federalism in the Philippines">federalism in the Philippines</a>. Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago" id="mwBVA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago">Miriam Defensor Santiago</a>, who almost won the presidency <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwBVE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">in 1992</a>, insists that she has recovered from cancer.<sup about="#mwt537" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-157"}}' id="cite_ref-157" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-157" id="mwBVI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBVM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBVQ">[</span>156<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBVU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Start_of_campaign_period_for_national_positions" data-mw-section-id="24" id="mwBVY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Start_of_campaign_period_for_national_positions">Start of campaign period for national positions</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=24" title="Edit section: Start of campaign period for national positions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBVc">Proclamation rallies were held by all five presidential tickets at the start of campaigning on February 9. The <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jejomar_Binay" id="mwBVg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jejomar Binay">Jejomar Binay</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gregorio_Honasan" id="mwBVk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gregorio Honasan">Gregorio Honasan</a> ticket started their campaign at a slum in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandaluyong" id="mwBVo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mandaluyong">Mandaluyong</a>, to "be with the Filipino poor". <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwBVs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwBVw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> started campaigning at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tondo,_Manila" id="mwBV0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tondo, Manila">Tondo, Manila</a>. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roxas_City" id="mwBV4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Roxas City">Roxas City</a>, the hometown of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwBV8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a>, was the host of the Roxas and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwBWA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> tandem's proclamation rally. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwBWE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Escudero" id="mwBWI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Escudero">Francis Escudero</a> had their proclamation rally at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_Miranda" id="mwBWM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Plaza Miranda">Plaza Miranda</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quiapo,_Manila" id="mwBWQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quiapo, Manila">Quiapo, Manila</a>. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Defensor_Santiago" id="mwBWU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Miriam Defensor Santiago">Miriam Defensor Santiago</a> held their proclamation rally at her running mate's <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwBWY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>' hometown of <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batac,_Ilocos_Norte" id="mwBWc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batac, Ilocos Norte">Batac</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Norte" id="mwBWg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Norte">Ilocos Norte</a>. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes" id="mwBWk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes">Antonio Trillanes</a> kicked off his campaign at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Santos" id="mwBWo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="General Santos">General Santos</a>.<sup about="#mwt540" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-158"}}' id="cite_ref-158" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-158" id="mwBWs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBWw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBW0">[</span>157<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBW4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBW8">On the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2016_–_Mindanao_leg" id="mwBXA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2016 – Mindanao leg">first presidential debate since 1992</a> on February 21 in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_de_Oro" id="mwBXE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan de Oro">Cagayan de Oro</a> hosted by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GMA_Network" id="mwBXI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="GMA Network">GMA Network</a>, the candidates commented that they were not given enough time to express their views.<sup about="#mwt543" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"roxastime"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-roxastime-159"}}' id="cite_ref-roxastime_159-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-roxastime-159" id="mwBXM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBXQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXU">[</span>158<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXY">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt546" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"commercialfree"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-commercialfree-160"}}' id="cite_ref-commercialfree_160-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-commercialfree-160" id="mwBXc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBXg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXk">[</span>159<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXo">]</span></span></a></sup> The Binay campaign lamented that the question on political dynasties was asked on him at a round where supposedly issues about <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwBXs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a> were the topics.<sup about="#mwt549" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"inappropriate"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-inappropriate-161"}}' id="cite_ref-inappropriate_161-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-inappropriate-161" id="mwBXw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBX0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBX4">[</span>160<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBX8">]</span></span></a></sup> Several weeks later, on March 8, the Supreme Court reversed the decision of the commission to disqualify Poe, thereby allowing her to run.<sup about="#mwt552" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-162"}}' id="cite_ref-162" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-162" id="mwBYA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBYE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYI">[</span>161<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBYQ">In the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2016_–_Mindanao_leg" id="mwBYU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2016 – Mindanao leg">Visayas leg of the presidential debate</a> held in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu_City" id="mwBYY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu City">Cebu City</a> hosted by <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TV5_(Philippines)" id="mwBYc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="TV5 (Philippines)">TV5</a>, the respective campaigns were generally more receptive of the conduct of the debate. The debate proper though was delayed due to a misunderstanding of the rules: Binay was informed by debate moderator <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luchi_Cruz-Valdez" id="mwBYg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Luchi Cruz-Valdez">Luchi Cruz-Valdez</a> that bringing of notes was allowed, but the commission ruled it out when the Roxas camp objected to it.<sup about="#mwt555" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-163"}}' id="cite_ref-163" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-163" id="mwBYk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBYo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYs">[</span>162<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Start_of_campaign_period_for_local_positions" data-mw-section-id="25" id="mwBY0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Start_of_campaign_period_for_local_positions">Start of campaign period for local positions</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=25" title="Edit section: Start of campaign period for local positions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBY4">Candidates started gaining endorsements from local politicians when campaigning for local positions started just right before <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Easter" id="mwBY8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Easter">Easter</a>. Poe got the endorsement of Manila Mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Estrada" id="mwBZA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Joseph Estrada">Joseph Estrada</a> over Binay, who was Estrada's running mate in 2010.<sup about="#mwt558" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"CNN"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-CNN-164"}}' id="cite_ref-CNN_164-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-CNN-164" id="mwBZE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBZI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZM">[</span>163<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZQ">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One_Cebu" id="mwBZU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="One Cebu">One Cebu</a> abandoned their original support for Binay to Duterte, vowing to deliver one million votes.<sup about="#mwt561" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-165"}}' id="cite_ref-165" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-165" id="mwBZY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBZc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZg">[</span>164<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZk">]</span></span></a></sup> Binay also lost the support of the Remullas of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cavite" id="mwBZo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cavite">Cavite</a> of which Cavite Governor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonvic_Remulla" id="mwBZs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jonvic Remulla">Jonvic</a> is his spokesperson, to Duterte.<sup about="#mwt564" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-166"}}' id="cite_ref-166" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-166" id="mwBZw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBZ0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZ4">[</span>165<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZ8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBaA">On an April 17 campaign rally, Duterte said on an Australian rape victim that "Was I mad because she was raped? Yes, that's one of the reasons. But she really was beautiful. The mayor should have been first."<sup about="#mwt567" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-167"}}' id="cite_ref-167" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-167" id="mwBaE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBaI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaM">[</span>166<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaQ">]</span></span></a></sup> This was met by condemnation both from his opponents,<sup about="#mwt570" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-168"}}' id="cite_ref-168" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-168" id="mwBaU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBaY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBac">[</span>167<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBag">]</span></span></a></sup> and other sectors of society, including the Australian ambassador to Manila.<sup about="#mwt573" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-169"}}' id="cite_ref-169" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-169" id="mwBak"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBao"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBas">[</span>168<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaw">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte apologized for the incident, blamed his "gutter language", but did not apologize for his remarks.<sup about="#mwt576" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-170"}}' id="cite_ref-170" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-170" id="mwBa0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBa4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBa8">[</span>169<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBbE">The third leg of the presidential debate was held in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pangasinan" id="mwBbI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pangasinan">Pangasinan</a> on April 24. When asked about the ongoing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Territorial_disputes_in_the_South_China_Sea" id="mwBbM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Territorial disputes in the South China Sea">South China Sea dispute</a>, Duterte said he will abide the decision of the arbitral tribunal in the <i id="mwBbQ"><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippines_v._China" id="mwBbU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippines v. China">Philippines v. China</a></i> case, and if tribunal rules in favor of the Philippines and China refuses to honor the decision, he will ride a <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Personal_watercraft" id="mwBbY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Personal watercraft">jetski</a> to the disputed islands to plant a Philippine flag.<sup about="#mwt579" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-171"}}' id="cite_ref-171" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-171" id="mwBbc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBbg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbk">[</span>170<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBbs">On April 26, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes" id="mwBbw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes">Antonio Trillanes</a> claims that Duterte has a bank account worth <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_peso" id="mwBb0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine peso">₱</a>211 million that wasn't declared in his statement of assets, liabilities and net worth. On the same day, Duterte denied the existence of the bank accounts, which were supposedly in a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_of_the_Philippine_Islands" id="mwBb4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bank of the Philippine Islands">Bank of the Philippine Islands</a> (BPI) branch in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Julia_Vargas_Avenue" id="mwBb8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Julia Vargas Avenue">Julia Vargas Avenue</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasig" id="mwBcA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasig">Pasig</a>, and was jointly held with his daughter <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwBcE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara</a>.<sup about="#mwt582" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-172"}}' id="cite_ref-172" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-172" id="mwBcI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBcM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcQ">[</span>171<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcU">]</span></span></a></sup> A day later, Duterte acknowledged the existence of the bank account at the BPI branch in Julia Vargas, but it had "only thousands" in funds. Duterte later challenged Trillanes to execute an affidavit stating how he got the information, and he'd promise to open the account.<sup about="#mwt585" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-173"}}' id="cite_ref-173" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-173" id="mwBcY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBcc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcg">[</span>172<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBck">]</span></span></a></sup> On May 2, Trillanes and Duterte's lawyer met at the BPI Julia Vargas branch. Duterte's lawyer brought with him a <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special_power_of_attorney" id="mwBco" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Special power of attorney">special power of attorney</a> requesting BPI to certify the existence of Duterte's account.<sup about="#mwt588" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-174"}}' id="cite_ref-174" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-174" id="mwBcs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBcw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBc0">[</span>173<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBc4">]</span></span></a></sup> In the end, no bank records were released, as the bank asked for more time to accede to the request. Trillanes expressed disappointment, as the special power of attorney invoked on whether the bank account asked for the current balance of the account, not the transaction history that he wanted.<sup about="#mwt591" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-175"}}' id="cite_ref-175" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-175" id="mwBc8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBdA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdE">[</span>174<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBdM">A week before the election, an anti-Duterte advertisement began appearing on television; it was already on rotation in radio stations. Paid for by Trillanes, the commercial features children reacting to Duterte's controversial remarks. The video shows several children remarking Duterte's controversial remarks and acts, including his cursing of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pope_Francis" id="mwBdQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pope Francis">Pope Francis</a> and the comments about the rape-slay on the Australian missionary.<sup about="#mwt595" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-176"}}' id="cite_ref-176" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-176" id="mwBdU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBdY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdc">[</span>175<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt598" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"ad01"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-ad01-177"}}' id="cite_ref-ad01_177-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-ad01-177" id="mwBdk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBdo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBds">[</span>176<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdw">]</span></span></a></sup> It was widely disapproved at social media.<sup about="#mwt601" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-178"}}' id="cite_ref-178" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-178" id="mwBd0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBd4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBd8">[</span>177<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBeA">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABS-CBN_Corporation" id="mwBeE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="ABS-CBN Corporation">ABS-CBN</a> defended their decision to air the ad, saying that "We are duty-bound to air a legitimate ad." A similar ad also aired on <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GMA_Network" id="mwBeI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="GMA Network">GMA Network</a>. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mwBeM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> said that the ad had been rejected by other TV networks, but was "blindly approved" by ABS-CBN's ethics committee.<sup about="#mwt604" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-179"}}' id="cite_ref-179" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-179" id="mwBeQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBeU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBeY">[</span>178<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBec">]</span></span></a></sup> He also accused President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benigno_Aquino_III" id="mwBeg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Benigno Aquino III">Benigno Aquino III</a> and two other rivals such as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwBek" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwBeo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABS-CBN_Corporation" id="mwBes" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="ABS-CBN Corporation">ABS-CBN</a> of making the ad.<sup about="#mwt608" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-180"}}' id="cite_ref-180" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-180" id="mwBew"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBe0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBe4">[</span>179<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBe8">]</span></span></a></sup> A <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taguig" id="mwBfA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Taguig">Taguig</a> court later disallowed the commercial to be aired, saying that "These advertisements do manifestly oppose a candidate and thus the court cannot allow minor children to be used in such black propaganda."<sup about="#mwt611" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-181"}}' id="cite_ref-181" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-181" id="mwBfE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBfI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBfM">[</span>180<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBfQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBfU">A few days before the election, Roxas called for Poe to back out, in order to defeat Duterte. Poe rebuffed Roxas' offer, saying that she would never withdraw from the presidential race.<sup about="#mwt614" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-182"}}' id="cite_ref-182" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-182" id="mwBfY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBfc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBfg">[</span>181<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBfk">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, President Aquino also called on Poe, Binay and Santiago to unite against Duterte. A Binay spokesperson retorted that instead of the president calling for unity against a specific candidate, Aquino should've just insured of clean elections.<sup about="#mwt617" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-183"}}' id="cite_ref-183" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-183" id="mwBfo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBfs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBfw">[</span>182<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBf0">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Miting_de_avance" data-mw-section-id="26" id="mwBf4"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Miting_de_avance">Miting de avance</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=26" title="Edit section: Miting de avance"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBf8">A tradition of Philippine politics, the "miting de avance" is the final political rally before the election itself. For the Duterte campaign, the miting de avance was held at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rizal_Park" id="mwBgA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rizal Park">Rizal Park</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila" id="mwBgE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila">Manila</a>; however, his campaign pointed to the Liberal Party of sabotaging the rally, as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Backhoe" id="mwBgI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Backhoe">backhoes</a> dug up holes in the park. The administrators of the park countered that they were only removing the asphalt from the Burnham green of the park.<sup about="#mwt620" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-184"}}' id="cite_ref-184" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-184" id="mwBgM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBgQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBgU">[</span>183<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBgY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBgc">Two days before the election, all mitings de avance were held in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwBgg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a>. The Santiago campaign held their "youth supporters rally" at West Triangle, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwBgk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>. The Roxas campaign held theirs at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_Memorial_Circle" id="mwBgo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon Memorial Circle">Quezon Memorial Circle</a> in the same city, while the Binay campaign held it at the corner of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalayaan_Avenue" id="mwBgs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kalayaan Avenue">Kalayaan Avenue</a> and Lawton Avenue in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwBgw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a>. Grace Poe held her miting de avance at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_Miranda" id="mwBg0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Plaza Miranda">Plaza Miranda</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila" id="mwBg4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila">Manila</a>. The Duterte campaign pushed through with their rally at Rizal Park.<sup about="#mwt623" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-185"}}' id="cite_ref-185" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-185" id="mwBg8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBhA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBhE">[</span>184<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBhI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Results" data-mw-section-id="27" id="mwBhM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Results">Results</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=27" title="Edit section: Results"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt624" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Congressional canvass for the 2016 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBhQ" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt625" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt624" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBhU" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congressional_canvass_for_the_2016_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congressional canvass for the 2016 Philippine presidential election">Congressional canvass for the 2016 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwBhY">Congress canvassed the results in record time of three days. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwBhc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> Mayor Rodrigo Duterte and Representative from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwBhg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a> Leni Robredo were proclaimed president and vice president on May 30, in front of a joint session, at the Batasang Pambansa Complex. Duterte notably skipped his proclamation, as he has consistently skipped his prior proclamations.<sup about="#mwt628" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Alberto-Masakayan-2016"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Alberto-Masakayan-2016-186"}}' id="cite_ref-Alberto-Masakayan-2016_186-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Alberto-Masakayan-2016-186" id="mwBhk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBho"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBhs">[</span>185<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBhw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<section aria-labelledby="For_president_2" data-mw-section-id="28" id="mwBh0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="For_president_2">For president</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=28" title="Edit section: For president"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwBh4" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" id="mwBh8"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwBiA" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png/250px-2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png/500px-2016_Philippine_presidential_election_provincial_results.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwBiE">As above, but showing the percentages of the winning candidate per province and city. Note that to avoid confusion with Roxas, the color used for Duterte here is red, which was his campaign color.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwBiI">All candidates except for Poe and Santiago carried their respective hometowns. Duterte carried the majority of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwBiM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a>, the so-called "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mega_Manila" id="mwBiQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mega Manila">Mega Manila</a>" (except Makati where Binay won) and the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebuano_language" id="mwBiU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebuano language">Cebuano-speaking provinces</a> in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visayas" id="mwBiY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Visayas">Visayas</a>, except Negros Oriental. Roxas carried the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hiligaynon_language" id="mwBic" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hiligaynon language">Ilonggo</a>-speaking provinces, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waray_language" id="mwBig" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Waray language">Waray</a> speaking provinces, Negros Oriental, his running mate's province of Camarines Sur and neighboring Albay in Bicol, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mimaropa" id="mwBik" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mimaropa">Mimaropa</a>. Poe carried most of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Region" id="mwBio" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Region">Ilocos Region</a>, except Ilocos Norte, including her father's home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pangasinan" id="mwBis" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pangasinan">Pangasinan</a>. Binay carried his parents' home region of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_Valley" id="mwBiw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan Valley">Cagayan Valley</a> and his hometown of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwBi0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a>, where he served as mayor for several decades. Santiago didn't win in any province.</p>

about="#mwt629" id="mwBjM">

</section></section><section aria-labelledby="For_vice_president_2" data-mw-section-id="31" id="mwCA4"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="For_vice_president_2">For vice president</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=31" title="Edit section: For vice president"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwCA8" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png" id="mwCBA"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwCBE" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png/250px-Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png/500px-Provincial_Breakdown_of_VP_Race_2016.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwCBI">Provincial and city breakdown of the vice presidential election.</figcaption></figure><p id="mwCBM">Robredo carried the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bicol_Region" id="mwCBQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bicol Region">Bicol Region</a>, except for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sorsogon" id="mwCBU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sorsogon">Sorsogon</a>, Escudero's home province, Roxas' stronghold of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Western_Visayas" id="mwCBY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Western Visayas">Western Visayas</a> and <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_(Philippines)" id="mwCBc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros (Philippines)">Negros</a>, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autonomous_Region_in_Muslim_Mindanao" id="mwCBg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao">Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Mindanao" id="mwCBk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Northern Mindanao">Northern Mindanao</a>, the entire <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mimaropa" id="mwCBo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mimaropa">Mimaropa</a> region, majority of provinces in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Visayas" id="mwCBs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Eastern Visayas">Eastern Visayas</a>, majority of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caraga" id="mwCBw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Caraga">Caraga</a> region, majority of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_Peninsula" id="mwCB0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga Peninsula">Zamboanga</a> region, <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_province" id="mwCB4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon province">Quezon province</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Visayas" id="mwCB8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Central Visayas">Central Visayas</a> less Cebu City, almost half of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soccsksargen" id="mwCCA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Soccsksargen">Soccsksargen</a>, and President Aquino's home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarlac" id="mwCCE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tarlac">Tarlac</a>. Marcos won the so-called "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_North" id="mwCCI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Solid North">Solid North</a>", the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwCCM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">National Capital Region</a> (except Taguig, where Cayetano won), his mother's home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leyte" id="mwCCQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leyte">Leyte</a>, and all provinces of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soccsksargen" id="mwCCU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Soccsksargen">Soccsksargen</a>. Cayetano won in his hometown of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taguig" id="mwCCY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Taguig">Taguig</a>, and in Duterte's home region in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Region" id="mwCCc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Region">Davao Region</a>.</p><figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwCCg" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" id="mwCCk"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwCCo" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png/250px-2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png/500px-2016_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_provincial_results.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwCCs">As above, but showing the percentages of the winning candidate per province and city. Note that to avoid confusion with Robredo, the color used for Cayetano here is red. To further avoid confusion with Cayetano, green is used for Marcos.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwCCw">Robredo's winning margin of 0.61% is the closest margin since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fernando_Lopez" id="mwCC0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Fernando Lopez">Fernando Lopez</a>'s victory in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1965_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCC4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1965 Philippine presidential election">1965 vice presidential election</a>. Marcos subsequently protested the results, which was unanimously struck down by the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Electoral_Tribunal" id="mwCC8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential Electoral Tribunal">Presidential Electoral Tribunal</a> on February 16, 2021.<sup about="#mwt715" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-188"}}' id="cite_ref-188" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-188" id="mwCDA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCDE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDI">[</span>186<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

</section><section aria-labelledby="Electoral_protest" data-mw-section-id="34" id="mwCmE"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Electoral_protest">Electoral protest</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=34" title="Edit section: Electoral protest"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCmI">On the evening of May 10, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwCmM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> took first place on the unofficial vote count led by the Parish Pastoral Council on Responsible Voting (PPCRV), and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwCmQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> suspected that the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwCmU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a> manipulated the vice presidential votes in favor of Robredo.<sup about="#mwt795" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-194"}}' id="cite_ref-194" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-194" id="mwCmY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCmc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCmg">[</span>187<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCmk">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos soon ordered the Commission on Elections to stop the unofficial count by the PPCRV, and he stated that no one has been aware that his lead on the unofficial vote count fell down.<sup about="#mwt798" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-195"}}' id="cite_ref-195" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-195" id="mwCmo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCms"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCmw">[</span>188<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCm0">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCm4">Marcos then filed an election protest to the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Electoral_Tribunal" id="mwCm8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential Electoral Tribunal">Presidential Electoral Tribunal</a> on June 29, 2016, the eve of the oath-taking of the newly elected officials. Marcos wanted a recount in 27 provinces and cities, in 36,000 precincts. Marcos sought for the explanation of supposed 3 million <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Undervotes" id="mwCnA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Undervotes">undervotes</a> which were allegedly "unaccounted for", which the Robredo camp said that is "normal".<sup about="#mwt801" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-196"}}' id="cite_ref-196" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-196" id="mwCnE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCnI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCnM">[</span>189<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCnQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo filed a counter-protest, which the tribunal rejected in September 2017. The tribunal also denied Marcos' first cause of action to nullify the result of the vice presidential election, because even if it is nullified, the tribunal ruled that it doesn't mean that he won.<sup about="#mwt804" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-197"}}' id="cite_ref-197" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-197" id="mwCnU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCnY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCnc">[</span>190<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCng">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCnk">On April 2, 2018, the manual recount of votes cast in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwCno" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo" id="mwCns" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo">Iloilo</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Oriental" id="mwCnw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Oriental">Negros Oriental</a>, the results of the election which were contested by former senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwCn0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>, started.<sup about="#mwt807" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Punay-2018"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Punay-2018-198"}}' id="cite_ref-Punay-2018_198-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Punay-2018-198" id="mwCn4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCn8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCoA">[</span>191<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCoE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt810" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Canlas-2018"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Canlas-2018-199"}}' id="cite_ref-Canlas-2018_199-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Canlas-2018-199" id="mwCoI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCoM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCoQ">[</span>192<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCoU">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt813" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Calayag"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Calayag-200"}}' id="cite_ref-Calayag_200-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Calayag-200" id="mwCoY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCoc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCog">[</span>193<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCok">]</span></span></a></sup> The recount was originally scheduled for February but was postponed to March 19, and then moved again to April 2.<sup about="#mwt814" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Canlas-2018"}}' id="cite_ref-Canlas-2018_199-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Canlas-2018-199" id="mwCoo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCos"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCow">[</span>192<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCo0">]</span></span></a></sup> The first day was plagued with issues of some ballots from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bato,_Camarines_Sur" id="mwCo4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bato, Camarines Sur">Bato, Camarines Sur</a> being drenched wet causing these to be intelligible, and missing audit cards. The Robredo camp said that these may have been damaged from previous floods and typhoons, and that the tribunal can go over to the ballot images, and reprint the audit logs from the records of the commission, while Marcos' lawyers said that it was recently doused over.<sup about="#mwt817" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-201"}}' id="cite_ref-201" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-201" id="mwCo8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCpA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpE">[</span>194<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpI">]</span></span></a></sup> Caguioa along with Senior Associate Justice Antonio Carpio were the only dissenters in a new resolution by the court ordering the parties to comment on the results of a recount of votes from Camarines Sur, Iloilo, and Negros Oriental.</p>
<p id="mwCpM">In October 2019, a resolution by the Supreme Court sitting as the Presidential Electoral Tribunal confirmed that Vice President Leni Robredo's lead over former senator Bongbong Marcos increased by 15,000 votes after it conducted a recount in three provinces chosen by Marcos.<sup about="#mwt820" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-202"}}' id="cite_ref-202" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-202" id="mwCpQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCpU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpY">[</span>195<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpc">]</span></span></a></sup> SC Associate Justice Benjamin Caguioa questioned the PET why it has not dismissed former senator Bongbong Marcos's election protest against Vice President Leni Robredo despite its "clear and unmistakable lack of basis."<sup about="#mwt823" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-203"}}' id="cite_ref-203" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-203" id="mwCpg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCpk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpo">[</span>196<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCps">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCpw">"Undoubtedly, protestant failed to make out his case. Why not apply Rule 65 now?" Caguioa said in a 7-page dissenting opinion. Rule 65 of the 2010 PET Rules allows a Protestant to choose not more than three provinces that "best exemplify" the fraud that he alleges happened in an election. Once ballots there are examined, the tribunal may dismiss the case if it is convinced that the Protestant will "most probably fail to make out his case."</p>
<p id="mwCp0">Marcos filed an inhibition (recusal) plea earlier against Associate Justice Benjamin Caguioa. However, the Supreme Court junked the plea, issuing a "stern warning" against the former senator that any unfounded and inappropriate accusation will be dealt with in the future with more severity.<sup about="#mwt826" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-204"}}' id="cite_ref-204" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-204" id="mwCp4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCp8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqA">[</span>197<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCqI">The Supreme Court sitting as the Presidential Electoral Tribunal also earlier upheld the 25-percent shading threshold, a main contention of Marcos's camp in their electoral protest.<sup about="#mwt829" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-205"}}' id="cite_ref-205" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-205" id="mwCqM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCqQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqU">[</span>198<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCqc">On February 16, 2021, the PET unanimously dismissed Bongbong Marcos' electoral protest against Vice President Leni Robredo.<sup about="#mwt832" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PETdismissalSCMP"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-PETdismissalSCMP-206"}}' id="cite_ref-PETdismissalSCMP_206-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PETdismissalSCMP-206" id="mwCqg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCqk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqo">[</span>199<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqs">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt835" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PETdismissal"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-PETdismissal-207"}}' id="cite_ref-PETdismissal_207-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PETdismissal-207" id="mwCqw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCq0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCq4">[</span>200<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCq8">]</span></span></a></sup> Media reports cited the reasons for the dismissal<sup about="#mwt836" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PETdecisionABSCBN"}}' id="cite_ref-PETdecisionABSCBN_208-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PETdecisionABSCBN-208" id="mwCrA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCrE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrI">[</span>201<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrM">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt837" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"TetchTorresTupasPETDecision"}}' id="cite_ref-TetchTorresTupasPETDecision_209-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-TetchTorresTupasPETDecision-209" id="mwCrQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCrU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrY">[</span>202<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrc">]</span></span></a></sup> as: "<i id="mwCrg">failure to allege specific acts showing electoral fraud</i>"; "<i id="mwCrk">allegations (which) were bare, laden with generic &amp; repetitious allegations, no critical information as to time, place and manner of irregularities,</i>” and "<i id="mwCro">the absence of substantial recovery of votes in the 3 pilot provinces Marcos had chosen where Robredo actually gained additional votes with 1,510,718 against Marcos’ 204,512.</i>"<sup about="#mwt840" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PETdecisionABSCBN"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-PETdecisionABSCBN-208"}}' id="cite_ref-PETdecisionABSCBN_208-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PETdecisionABSCBN-208" id="mwCrs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCrw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCr0">[</span>201<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCr4">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt843" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"TetchTorresTupasPETDecision"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-TetchTorresTupasPETDecision-209"}}' id="cite_ref-TetchTorresTupasPETDecision_209-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-TetchTorresTupasPETDecision-209" id="mwCr8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCsA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsE">[</span>202<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Results_per_province" data-mw-section-id="35" id="mwCsM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Results_per_province">Results per province</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=35" title="Edit section: Results per province"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwCsQ" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_NLE_Turnout.png" id="mwCsU"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="1916" decoding="async" height="407" id="mwCsY" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2016_NLE_Turnout.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/2016_NLE_Turnout.png/250px-2016_NLE_Turnout.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/2016_NLE_Turnout.png/500px-2016_NLE_Turnout.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwCsc">Turnout per province</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwCsg">Each province and city that is independent of a province and is a legislative district by itself sends its certificates of canvass to Congress. Each diplomatic post, local absentee voters and detainee voters also send their respective certificates of canvass. From there, the results are tallied in a joint session of Congress.</p>
<p id="mwCsk">Notably, most candidates won in their home regions, provinces or cities. Duterte (Davao Region), Roxas (Western Visayas), Binay (Makati)  Robredo (Camarines Sur), Marcos (Ilocos Region), Cayetano (Taguig) and Escudero (Sorsogon) won in their respective strongholds. Roxas, Santiago, Cayetano and Robredo were also helped by votes from their respective running mates' strongholds (Camarines Sur, Ilocos Region, Davao Region and Western Visayas, respectively). Binay, Poe, Duterte and Marcos were also helped from votes from which their families originated (Cagayan Valley, Pangasinan, Cebu and Leyte, respectively). Duterte was particularly helped by his big winning margins in Mindanao, where he polled more than 60% of the vote in some places.</p>
<p id="mwCso">For the presidential election, the following candidates won in these places:</p>
<ul id="mwCss"><li id="mwCsw">Duterte: Most of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwCs0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Visayas" id="mwCs4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Central Visayas">Central Visayas</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leyte" id="mwCs8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leyte">Leyte</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biliran" id="mwCtA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Biliran">Biliran</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwCtE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calabarzon" id="mwCtI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Calabarzon">Calabarzon</a> (except Quezon), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bataan" id="mwCtM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bataan">Bataan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baguio" id="mwCtQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Baguio">Baguio</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bulacan" id="mwCtU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bulacan">Bulacan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Norte" id="mwCtY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Norte">Ilocos Norte</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pampanga" id="mwCtc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pampanga">Pampanga</a></li>
<li id="mwCtg">Roxas, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Western_Visayas" id="mwCtk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Western Visayas">Western Visayas</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Island_Region" id="mwCto" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Island Region">Negros Island Region</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samar" id="mwCts" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Samar">Samar Island</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mimaropa" id="mwCtw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mimaropa">Mimaropa</a> (except Oriental Mindoro and Marinduque), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albay" id="mwCt0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Albay">Albay</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwCt4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agusan_del_Sur" id="mwCt8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Agusan del Sur">Agusan del Sur</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masbate" id="mwCuA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Masbate">Masbate</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camiguin" id="mwCuE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camiguin">Camiguin</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misamis_Occidental" id="mwCuI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Misamis Occidental">Misamis Occidental</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batanes" id="mwCuM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batanes">Batanes</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarlac" id="mwCuQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tarlac">Tarlac</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_del_Norte" id="mwCuU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga del Norte">Zamboanga del Norte</a></li>
<li id="mwCuY">Poe: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Region" id="mwCuc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Region">Ilocos Region</a> (except Ilocos Norte), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(province)" id="mwCug" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aurora (province)">Aurora</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Norte" id="mwCuk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Norte">Camarines Norte</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ifugao" id="mwCuo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ifugao">Ifugao</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mountain_Province" id="mwCus" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mountain Province">Mountain Province</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nueva_Ecija" id="mwCuw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nueva Ecija">Nueva Ecija</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nueva_Vizcaya" id="mwCu0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nueva Vizcaya">Nueva Vizcaya</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pangasinan" id="mwCu4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pangasinan">Pangasinan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon" id="mwCu8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon">Quezon</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_del_Sur" id="mwCvA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga del Sur">Zamboanga del Sur</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zambales" id="mwCvE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zambales">Zambales</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oriental_Mindoro" id="mwCvI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Oriental Mindoro">Oriental Mindoro</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marinduque" id="mwCvM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Marinduque">Marinduque</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catanduanes" id="mwCvQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Catanduanes">Catanduanes</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sorsogon" id="mwCvU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sorsogon">Sorsogon</a></li>
<li id="mwCvY">Binay: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_Valley" id="mwCvc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan Valley">Cagayan Valley</a> (except Batanes and Nueva Vizcaya), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abra_(province)" id="mwCvg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Abra (province)">Abra</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apayao" id="mwCvk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Apayao">Apayao</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalinga_(province)" id="mwCvo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kalinga (province)">Kalinga</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwCvs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a></li>
<li id="mwCvw">Santiago: Although Santiago didn't win a province, her largest shares of the vote came from Baguio, Benguet, Ilocos Norte and Iloilo.</li></ul>
<p id="mwCv0">For the vice presidential election, the following candidates won in these places:</p>
<ul id="mwCv4"><li id="mwCv8">Robredo, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bicol_Region" id="mwCwA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bicol Region">Bicol Region</a> (except Sorsogon), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visayas" id="mwCwE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Visayas">Visayas</a> (except <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu_City" id="mwCwI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu City">Cebu City</a>, Mandaue, Biliran and Leyte), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batangas" id="mwCwM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batangas">Batangas</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batanes" id="mwCwQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batanes">Batanes</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon" id="mwCwU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon">Quezon</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarlac" id="mwCwY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tarlac">Tarlac</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Mindanao" id="mwCwc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Northern Mindanao">Northern Mindanao</a> (except Cagayan de Oro and Iligan). <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_Peninsula" id="mwCwg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga Peninsula">Zamboanga Peninsula</a> (except Zamboanga del Sur), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autonomous_Region_in_Muslim_Mindanao" id="mwCwk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao">Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao</a> (except Sulu). <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caraga" id="mwCwo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Caraga">Caraga</a> (except for Dinagat Islands and Surigao del Sur)</li>
<li id="mwCws">Marcos: The "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_North" id="mwCww" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Solid North">Solid North</a>" part of Luzon (except Batanes), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Luzon" id="mwCw0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Central Luzon">Central Luzon</a> (except Tarlac), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwCw4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a> (except Taguig), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_de_Oro" id="mwCw8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan de Oro">Cagayan de Oro</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soccsksargen" id="mwCxA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Soccsksargen">Soccsksargen</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dinagat_Islands" id="mwCxE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Dinagat Islands">Dinagat Islands</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sulu" id="mwCxI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sulu">Sulu</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_del_Sur" id="mwCxM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga del Sur">Zamboanga del Sur</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leyte" id="mwCxQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leyte">Leyte</a>.</li>
<li id="mwCxU">Cayetano: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Region" id="mwCxY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Region">Davao Region</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu_City" id="mwCxc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu City">Cebu City</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandaue" id="mwCxg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mandaue">Mandaue</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Butuan" id="mwCxk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Butuan">Butuan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iligan" id="mwCxo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iligan">Iligan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taguig" id="mwCxs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Taguig">Taguig</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surigao_del_Sur" id="mwCxw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Surigao del Sur">Surigao del Sur</a></li>
<li id="mwCx0">Escudero: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sorsogon" id="mwCx4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sorsogon">Sorsogon</a></li></ul>
<p id="mwCx8"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Oriental" id="mwCyA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Oriental">Negros Oriental</a>'s winning streak of being the presidential <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bellwether_(politics)" id="mwCyE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bellwether (politics)">bellwether</a> that started in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1969_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCyI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1969 Philippine presidential election">1969</a> ended in this election, when Roxas won there, but Duterte won the national count. It was replaced by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilan" id="mwCyM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Basilan">Basilan</a>, which now has the longest winning streak, starting in <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1981_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCyQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1981 Philippine presidential election">1981</a>.</p>

<section aria-labelledby="Close_provinces_and_cities" data-mw-section-id="-1" id="mwC2A"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Close_provinces_and_cities">Close provinces and cities</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=36" title="Edit section: Close provinces and cities"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Margin of victory is less than 5% for the presidential election:</p>
<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albay" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Albay">Albay</a>: 0.03% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oriental_Mindoro" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Oriental Mindoro">Oriental Mindoro</a>: 0.06% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_del_Norte" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga del Norte">Zamboanga del Norte</a>: 0.10% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ifugao" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ifugao">Ifugao</a>: 0.20% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batangas" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batangas">Batangas</a>: 0.21% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bataan" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bataan">Bataan</a>: 0.34% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nueva_Vizcaya" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nueva Vizcaya">Nueva Vizcaya</a>: 0.88% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Sur">Ilocos Sur</a>: 1.05% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Union" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="La Union">La Union</a>: 1.27% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Navotas" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Navotas">Navotas</a>: 1.46% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarlac" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tarlac">Tarlac</a>: 1.50% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_del_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga del Sur">Zamboanga del Sur</a>: 2.15% (Poe win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quirino" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quirino">Quirino</a>: 2.52% (Binay win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Samar" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Eastern Samar">Eastern Samar</a>: 3.46% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samar_(province)" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Samar (province)">Samar</a>: 3.68% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>: 3.7% (Roxas win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mountain_Province" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mountain Province">Mountain Province</a>: 4.86% (Poe win)</li></ul>
<p>Margin of victory is less than 5% for the vice presidential election:</p>
<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surigao_del_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Surigao del Sur">Surigao del Sur</a>: 0.01% (Cayetano wins by 16 votes)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Occidental" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Occidental">Davao Occidental</a>: 0.45% (Cayetano win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lanao_del_Norte" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lanao del Norte">Lanao del Norte</a>: 0.52% (Robredo win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu_City" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu City">Cebu City</a>: 1.05% (Cayetano win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samar" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Samar">Samar</a>: 1.24% (Robredo win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_de_Oro" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan de Oro">Cagayan de Oro</a>: 1.38% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marikina" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Marikina">Marikina</a>: 1.68% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parañaque" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Parañaque">Parañaque</a>: 2.26% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_City" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga City">Zamboanga City</a>: 2.39% (Robredo win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Cotabato" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="South Cotabato">South Cotabato</a>: 2.51% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cotabato" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cotabato">Cotabato</a>: 2.95% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laguna_(province)" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Laguna (province)">Laguna</a>: 4.07% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tarlac" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tarlac">Tarlac</a>: 4.99% (Robredo win)</li></ul></section></td>
<td class="col-break col-break-2">
<section aria-labelledby="Landslides" data-mw-section-id="-1" id="mwC2E"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Landslides">Landslides</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2016_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=37" title="Edit section: Landslides"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Margin of victory is more than 50% for the presidential election:</p>
<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a>: 95.18% (Duterte wins in his home city)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_del_Norte" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao del Norte">Davao del Norte</a>: 89.35% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_del_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao del Sur">Davao del Sur</a>: 87.28% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Occidental" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Occidental">Davao Occidental</a>: 80.73% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Oriental" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Oriental">Davao Oriental</a>: 73.86% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lanao_del_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lanao del Sur">Lanao del Sur</a>: 68.31% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capiz" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Capiz">Capiz</a>: 66.97% (Roxas wins in his home province)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sulu" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sulu">Sulu</a>: 66.45% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cotabato" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cotabato">Cotabato</a>: 62.26% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compostela_Valley" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Compostela Valley">Compostela Valley</a>: 59.73% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Absentee_voter" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Absentee voter">Absentee voters</a>: 59.59% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iligan" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iligan">Iligan</a>: 57.56% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarangani" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sarangani">Sarangani</a>: 54.64% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bukidnon" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bukidnon">Bukidnon</a>: 53.93% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cagayan_de_Oro" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cagayan de Oro">Cagayan de Oro</a>: 52.70% (Duterte win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camiguin" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camiguin">Camiguin</a>: 50.73% (Roxas win)</li></ul>
<p>Margin of victory is more than 50% for the vice presidential election:</p>
<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Norte" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Norte">Ilocos Norte</a>: 95.62% (Marcos wins in his home province)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Sur">Ilocos Sur</a>: 88.89% (Marcos win)</li>

<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Union" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="La Union">La Union</a>: 84.57% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>: 80.26% (Robredo wins in her home province)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apayao" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Apayao">Apayao</a>: 79.45% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camiguin" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camiguin">Camiguin</a>: 69.68% (Robredo win)</li>
